title: SessionLogWriter: implement per-session log files
description: Per-session log files under .sidekick/sessions/{id}/logs/ as the source of truth, replacing full-aggregate scan in the Timeline API. Implementation COMPLETE on main via PR #160 (e534c542, 2026-04-04). Remaining work: post-shipping bug fixes identified by adversarial plan review (e9d4). Plan docs: docs/plans/2026-04-03-per-session-logging-design.md and docs/plans/2026-04-03-per-session-logging-impl.md.
design: All 6 implementation tasks completed on aaab9092: PathResolver.sessionLogsDir(), SessionLogWriter class with LRU eviction, logEvent binding, daemon init, CLI init, Timeline API with aggregate fallback. Gap: E2E verification, bindSessionId wiring check, stress tests, docs.
acceptance_criteria: Build passes. Typecheck passes. Lint passes. Tests pass including new stress tests. PR merged.
close_reason: Epic complete. PR #176 merged to main. All implementation tasks done, completion gate passed, Copilot review addressed.

title: Staged reminder detail panel should show reminder text at time of staging
description: All 'Staged' reminder events should display consistent detail in the slide-out panel, including the full text of the reminder as it existed at staging time. Since reminder text can be dynamic (e.g. persona text, user-profile content), the actual rendered text must be captured at staging time and stored with the event. The detail panel format should be uniform across all staged reminder types. Implementation note: discuss capture mechanism when working this bead — may need to snapshot the reminder body into the event payload at emission time.
close_reason: Verified working in current build - reminderText populated in sidekickd.4.log. Earlier Playwright test showed stale events from pre-rebuild.

title: Staged user-profile reminder never shows consumed event in UI
description: The 'Staged: user-profile' reminder appears in the timeline but there is no corresponding 'Consumed: user-profile' event visible. Either the reminder is never consumed (leak), or the consumption event is not being emitted/logged. Investigate whether the user-profile reminder is actually delivered and consumed during hook execution, and ensure the consumed event is emitted and visible in the timeline.
close_reason: Closed

title: Snarky message events missing labels — should show 'Snarky Message Start/Finish' with message text
description: Snarky message events in the timeline do not display proper labels. The start bubble should say 'Snarky Message Start' and the finish bubble should say 'Snarky Message Finish' along with the generated message text (or a truncated preview of it). Currently these may be showing generic or missing labels in the timeline.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #135 (commit c4af225) — beads were left open after merge

title: Statusline events show 'session summary' instead of 'Statusline called' and lack input/output detail
description: Statusline timeline events currently display misleading text. They should: (a) show 'Statusline called' as the bubble label (not 'session summary' or similar), (b) include the hook input data in the detail panel (same format as hook:received events), and (c) include the output — the actual rendered statusline text string that was returned. The statusline hook script is called by Claude Code's statusLine hook, so it should follow the same input/output pattern as other hooks.
close_reason: Restored renderedText and hookInput fields to statusline:rendered events. PR #143 erroneously removed them citing a type mismatch that didn't exist.

title: Double session analysis triggered after first prompt — second deferred due to spurious first
description: After the first user prompt post-clear, two session analysis cycles fire. The first is triggered by the spurious 'bulk transcript replay complete' event (see related bead gca). The second (from UserPromptSubmit) arrives while the first is running, producing a 'Skip session analysis — Analysis deferred — will rerun after current analysis completes' decision. The second analysis then runs after the first finishes. Root cause is likely the spurious bulk replay (gca), but even if the first analysis is legitimate, the ordering/deduplication behavior needs review — the UI shows confusing back-to-back analysis cycles.
notes: PR #146 open. Fix adds isBuiltinCommandInvocation() to transcript service. 3 new tests, 2 updated. Build/typecheck/lint/tests clean. Code review and simplification in progress.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #146. Added isExcludedBuiltinCommandInvocation() to transcript service to suppress UserPrompt events for excluded builtin commands like /clear. Eliminates double session analysis after first prompt post-clear.

title: Missing bulk transcript replay start/finish events and spurious replay after clear
description: After the first user prompt following a /clear, a decision event appears: 'Run session analysis' with reason 'Bulk transcript replay complete — running catch-up analysis'. But (a) there are no corresponding bulk-transcript-replay:start or bulk-transcript-replay:finish events in the timeline, and (b) a bulk replay should not occur after a /clear since there is no prior transcript to replay. Investigate why the bulk replay path is triggered from a clean context and ensure replay lifecycle events are emitted if replays do occur.
close_reason: Closed

title: Duplicate remember-your-persona and user-profile reminders after clear
description: After a /clear command, the timeline shows multiple repeated remember-your-persona and user-profile staged reminders all within the same second. Two issues: (a) investigate why duplicates are emitted — the SessionStart hook should not stage the same reminder multiple times, and (b) timestamps need millisecond precision so events within the same second can be properly ordered in the UI timeline.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] stageToolsForFiles inner loop has no error handling — unhandled throw kills staging
description: In track-verification-tools.ts, the stageToolsForFiles inner loop (per-file, per-tool) has no try/catch. If ensureToolReminderStaged or stageReminder throws, the entire handler fails silently. Fix: wrap the per-tool staging in try/catch with logger.warn, skip the current tool and continue. File: packages/feature-reminders/src/handlers/staging/track-verification-tools.ts
notes:
## Acceptance Criteria
- Per-tool staging inside stageToolsForFiles wrapped in try/catch with logger.warn
- On error, current tool is skipped and loop continues to next tool
- Handler does not silently fail — partial staging succeeds rather than all-or-nothing
- Unit test covers: one tool throws during staging, other tools still staged successfully, error logged

## Workflow Requirements
1. All work MUST be done in an isolated git worktree (isolation: worktree on Agent tool)
2. Create a feature branch (fix/ prefix) and open a PR to main — NEVER commit directly to main
3. Before claiming done, run: pnpm build && pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint
4. Run the specific relevant tests: pnpm --filter @sidekick/feature-reminders test
5. After PR is created, invoke /wait-for-pr-comments to monitor Copilot review
6. Address ALL review feedback from Copilot — do not close with unresolved comments
7. Only after review completes or times out AND all fixes are applied can the bead be closed

## Memory Protocol
**On claim**: Run `bd memories` to check for learnings from previously completed beads in this epic (xp4). Apply any relevant patterns.
**On close**: Use `bd remember` to capture any reusable patterns, gotchas, or surprises discovered during the work. Include: what worked, what was tricky, what a future agent should know. Use `bd close` with a `--reason` that summarizes the key insight.
close_reason: Added try/catch around per-tool staging in stageToolsForFiles inner loop. On error, logs warning with tool details and continues to next tool. 3 new tests covering: partial staging success when one tool throws, warning logged, and state still written. PR #130.

title: [bug] createLoggerFacade.flush() silently drops buffered log entries
description: In structured-logging.ts, createLoggerFacade.flush() does not replay the pre-upgrade buffer when called before upgrade completes. Log entries buffered during early initialization are silently lost. Fix: flush should replay buffer entries to the active logger regardless of upgrade state, or at minimum write them to stderr. File: packages/sidekick-core/src/structured-logging.ts
notes:
## Acceptance Criteria
- flush() replays buffered log entries even when called before upgrade completes
- Buffered entries are written to the active logger (or stderr as fallback) — never silently dropped
- Unit test covers: flush pre-upgrade replays buffer, flush post-upgrade is no-op, buffer cleared after replay
- No regression in normal upgrade-then-log flow
- **ALSO FIX (from merged PR #131 post-merge review)**: LogEvents.reminderStaged must include `reminderText?: string` in its state param and payload — callers pass it for timeline details, currently silently dropped
- **ALSO FIX**: LogEvents.statuslineRendered metadata must include `renderedText` and `hookInput` optional fields — callers pass these for observability, currently silently dropped
- Both fixes use conditional spreads to avoid emitting undefined

## Workflow Requirements
1. All work MUST be done in an isolated git worktree (isolation: worktree on Agent tool)
2. Create a feature branch (fix/ prefix) and open a PR to main — NEVER commit directly to main
3. Before claiming done, run: pnpm build && pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint
4. Run the specific relevant tests: pnpm --filter @sidekick/core test -- --testPathPattern structured-logging
5. Invoke the `simplify` Skill tool on changed files — address all findings
6. Commit fix-ups from simplify, then push and create PR to main using gh pr create
7. After PR is created, invoke /wait-for-pr-comments to monitor Copilot review
8. Address ALL review feedback from Copilot — do not close with unresolved comments
9. Only after review completes or times out AND all fixes are applied can the bead be closed

## Memory Protocol (MANDATORY — not optional)
**On claim**: Run `bd memories` and review these specific memories:
- `xp4-barrel-reexport-pattern` — structured-logging.ts was recently refactored; LogEvents extracted to log-events.ts with re-exports
- `xp4-simplify-finds-real-issues` — simplify is mandatory, not ceremonial

**Before close (HARD REQUIREMENT)**: Run `bd remember "h8a: [key insight]"` capturing what was learned. Then `bd close` with `--reason`. Skipping this is a workflow violation.
close_reason: Fixed flush() silent log loss, restored reminderText and renderedText/hookInput fields dropped during PR #131 extraction. PR #137.

title: [bug] promptYesNo in uninstall.ts hangs if stdin closes without newline

Fix: replace with the readline-based pattern used elsewhere, or better yet, use the shared promptConfirm utility (once extracted via the CLI utilities bead). File: packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/uninstall.ts
notes:
## Acceptance Criteria
- promptYesNo replaced with readline-based implementation that handles stdin close/EOF gracefully
- Promise resolves (not hangs) when stdin closes without newline
- Unit test covers: normal yes/no input, EOF without newline, default answer behavior
- No other prompt implementations in uninstall.ts remain (use shared utility if available)

2. Create a feature branch (fix/ prefix) and open a PR to main — NEVER commit directly to main

5. After PR is created, invoke /wait-for-pr-comments to monitor Copilot review
6. Address ALL review feedback from Copilot — do not close with unresolved comments
7. Only after review completes or times out AND all fixes are applied can the bead be closed

## Memory Protocol
**On claim**: Run `bd memories` to check for learnings from previously completed beads in this epic (xp4). Apply any relevant patterns.
**On close**: Use `bd remember` to capture any reusable patterns, gotchas, or surprises discovered during the work. Include: what worked, what was tricky, what a future agent should know. Use `bd close` with a `--reason` that summarizes the key insight.

These are not real build errors — pnpm build/typecheck pass clean. The noise drowns out real diagnostics and wastes time triaging every session. Separate from 73t (worktree-specific) — this happens in the main working tree too. Root cause likely: tsconfig project references, pnpm workspace resolution, or missing typeRoots config. Acceptance criteria: zero false 'Cannot find module' diagnostics in VS Code / LSP when the project is open.

title: New session sometimes fails to show persona until first user prompt submit
description:
**Bug**: On new session start, the statusline renders before SessionStart hook completes, showing a persona-less default ('empty_summary' display mode) instead of the selected persona and snarky comment.

**Root Cause**: Claude Code fires the statusline command and SessionStart hook in parallel. The statusline creates its own StateService and reads session-persona.json directly from the filesystem. On a new session, this file doesn't exist until the SessionStart handler (P80 persona selection) writes it — which happens ~168ms+ after the statusline has already rendered.

**Evidence** (session a3662aa4-76b6-4e1e-a81e-5589850bc272):
- T+0ms: StatuslineRendered with displayMode=empty_summary (no persona)
- T+168ms: SessionStart HookReceived (begins processing)
- T+9928ms: StatuslineRendered with displayMode=session_summary (persona visible after first prompt)

**Scope**: Statusline display only — the system prompt persona injection (via reminders) works correctly since it's consumed during UserPromptSubmit, which fires after SessionStart completes.

**Acceptance**: New session statusline shows persona name and snarky comment on first render (or gracefully handles the race). Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
close_reason: Fixed: statusline polls for persona file on new session start (25ms interval, 1s timeout). Verified in production: 3/3 new sessions show persona on first render. Typical wait 200-364ms.

title: Doctor --fix --only=gitignore cannot repair missing gitignore section (hangs)
description: 'pnpm sidekick doctor --fix --only=gitignore' hangs when the sidekick gitignore section is missing, making it impossible to auto-repair. This is the same underlying hang as the doctor detect hang (git scanning untracked .sidekick/ files), but specifically blocks the --only=gitignore scoped fix path. The fix operation should either: (a) skip git operations when checking gitignore, or (b) repair gitignore first before any git status calls. Discovered during manual test 2.4.
close_reason: Manually verified passing — doctor --fix correctly repairs gitignore section and --only flag scopes correctly

title: Doctor hangs when gitignore sidekick section is missing
description: When the '# >>> sidekick' gitignore section is removed from .gitignore, 'pnpm sidekick doctor' and 'pnpm sidekick doctor --fix' both hang indefinitely (timeout after 30s). Root cause: the sidekick gitignore section hides .sidekick/logs/, .sidekick/sessions/, .sidekick/state/, etc. When removed, a git operation inside doctor suddenly scans thousands of untracked files in those directories, causing the hang. This blocks both detection (test 2.2) and scoped fix (test 2.4 --fix --only=gitignore). Discovered during manual tests 2.2 and 2.4.
close_reason: Manually verified passing — doctor --fix correctly repairs gitignore section and --only flag scopes correctly

title: Doctor crashes on corrupted user setup-status.json instead of graceful handling
description: SetupStatusService.getUserStatus() does not catch JSON.parse errors. When ~/.sidekick/setup-status.json contains invalid JSON, doctor crashes with [FATAL] SyntaxError instead of reporting 'needs attention' gracefully. Stack trace: JSON.parse → SetupStatusService.getUserStatus → SetupStatusService.runDoctorCheck. Expected: exit 1 with 'needs attention' message and no stack trace. Actual: [FATAL] crash with full stack trace. Discovered during manual test 1.3.
close_reason: Fixed: getUserStatus() and getProjectStatus() now catch SyntaxError from JSON.parse on corrupt files, log a warning, and return null (same as schema-invalid files). All 160 setup-status-service tests pass. Manual test 1.3 confirmed fixed.

title: uninstall --dry-run executes real uninstall: dry-run flag dropped in parseArgs return
description:
## Bug

`pnpm sidekick uninstall --dry-run` performs a real uninstall instead of a dry run. The `--dry-run` flag is silently dropped.

## Root Cause

`cli.ts:parseArgs()` (line 152) uses yargs-parser to parse all CLI flags, including `--dry-run` which is correctly declared in `CLI_OPTIONS.boolean` (line 116). However, the return statement at line 179-207 manually constructs the `ParsedArgs` object and **omits the `dry-run` field**. 

The `routeCommand` handler at line 576 reads `parsed['dry-run']` which is always `undefined`, so `Boolean(undefined)` is `false`, and the uninstall proceeds destructively.

## Confirmed

```
> initializeRuntime({ argv: ['uninstall', '--dry-run'] }).parsed['dry-run']
undefined
```

## Fix Required

Add `'dry-run': Boolean(parsed['dry-run'])` to the return object in `parseArgs()` (around line 196).

## Files
- `packages/sidekick-cli/src/cli.ts` — `parseArgs()` return object (line 179-207)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
- `pnpm sidekick uninstall --dry-run` shows what would be removed without actually removing anything
- Output includes `[dry-run]` prefix in the report

title: setup --check rejected as unrecognized option by CLI arg parser
description:
## Bug

Running `sidekick setup --check` fails with:
```
Error: Unrecognized option(s): --check
```

Despite the help text documenting `--check` as a valid option.

## Root Cause

`cli.ts:106-119` defines `CLI_OPTIONS.boolean` which feeds into `KNOWN_KEYS` (line 140). The `--check` flag is missing from this list. The strict validator at line 163-167 rejects any flag not in `KNOWN_KEYS` before the command reaches the setup handler.

The setup handler at `cli.ts:558` tries to read it as a positional arg (`parsed._?.[1] === '--check'`), which would never work anyway since yargs-parser consumes flags before they reach positionals.

## Fix Required

1. Add `'check'` to `CLI_OPTIONS.boolean` array (line 107-119)
2. Update the setup command routing at `cli.ts:558` to read from `parsed.check` instead of positional args

## Files
- `packages/sidekick-cli/src/cli.ts` — `CLI_OPTIONS.boolean` (line 107) and setup routing (line 558)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
- `pnpm sidekick setup --check` runs the doctor/check mode successfully
- `pnpm sidekick doctor` continues to work as before (alias)

title: dev-mode disable removes .gitignore entries even when plugin is installed
description:
## Bug

When the sidekick plugin is installed at user level (and possibly project level), running `dev-mode enable` then `dev-mode disable` removes the `.gitignore` entries for `.sidekick/`. The plugin still needs those entries.

## Root Cause

`doDisable()` in `dev-mode.ts:524-533` checks `setupService.isPluginInstalled()` before removing gitignore entries. This reads `pluginDetected` from `setup-status.json` (both user and project). However, `pluginDetected` is only written during the `setup` flow (`plugin-installer.ts`). If the plugin was installed outside of the setup flow (e.g., via `--plugin-dir`, or at user scope before this project ran setup), the flag is never set, and `isPluginInstalled()` returns false.

The guard at dev-mode.ts:525 exists but is ineffective because the data it depends on is stale or missing.

## Possible Fixes

1. **Detect plugin at disable time**: Instead of relying on the `pluginDetected` status flag, actively check whether the plugin is currently installed (e.g., check `~/.claude/plugins/` directory or use `claude plugin list` if available)
2. **Conservative default**: If `pluginDetected` is undefined/missing (vs explicitly false), assume plugin may be installed and preserve gitignore
3. **Set pluginDetected during session hooks**: The SessionStart hook already runs for plugin-installed sessions — it could set `pluginDetected: true`

## Files
- `packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/dev-mode.ts` — `doDisable()` gitignore guard (line 524-533)
- `packages/sidekick-core/src/setup-status-service.ts` — `isPluginInstalled()` (line 744)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
- After `dev-mode disable`, `.gitignore` entries are preserved when sidekick plugin is installed at any scope
- After `dev-mode disable`, `.gitignore` entries are removed when no plugin is installed

title: Incomplete vitest 4.x upgrade: workspace package.json specifiers still on ^2.x
description: The vitest upgrade commit (8c47265) updated the root package.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, and test files but missed updating vitest specifiers in all packages/*/package.json files. They still say ^2.1.8 / ^2.1.1 instead of ^4.0.18. Similarly, packages/sidekick-ui/package.json still has vite ^5.0.0 (should be ^6.x or ^7.x) and @vitejs/plugin-react ^4.2.0 (should be ^5.x). Running pnpm install in any fresh worktree regenerates the lockfile with downgrades, causing dirty working trees. Fix: update all workspace package.json vitest/vite specifiers to match the actual resolved versions.

title: dev-mode disable should kill running daemon processes (like dev-mode clean does)
description:
## Bug

When `pnpm sidekick dev-mode disable` runs, it removes hooks and cleans up settings but does NOT stop any running daemon processes for the project. The user is left with orphaned daemon processes that were started during the dev-mode session.

`dev-mode clean` already handles killing daemon processes, but `dev-mode disable` doesn't invoke that logic.

## Expected Behavior

`dev-mode disable` should kill any running daemon processes for the project before removing hooks/settings, matching what `dev-mode clean` does.

## Files
- `packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/dev-mode.ts` — `doDisable()` needs to call daemon cleanup logic that `doClean()` already uses

## Acceptance Criteria
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
- After `pnpm sidekick dev-mode disable`, no orphaned daemon processes remain for the project
- `dev-mode clean` behavior unchanged

title: dev-mode enable: SKILL.md npx→pnpm transform regex doesn't match actual content
description:
## Bug

When `dev-mode.sh enable` runs, `copySkillForDev()` in `packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/dev-mode.ts:128` applies this regex:

```typescript
content.replace(/npx @sidekick\/cli/g, 'pnpm sidekick')
```

But the actual SKILL.md (`packages/sidekick-plugin/skills/sidekick-config/SKILL.md`) uses `npx @scotthamilton77/sidekick`, not `npx @sidekick/cli`. The regex never matches, so the dev-mode copy retains npx invocations that won't work in dev context.

## Impact

In dev-mode, the sidekick-config skill tells Claude to run `npx @scotthamilton77/sidekick` commands, which fetches from npm instead of using the local build. This defeats the purpose of dev-mode.

## Fix Required

1. Update the regex in `copySkillForDev()` to match `npx @scotthamilton77/sidekick` → `pnpm sidekick`
2. The source SKILL.md must keep using `npx @scotthamilton77/sidekick` (correct for plugin installs)
3. The disable path (`removeDevSkill()`) already correctly removes the skill directory — no changes needed there

## Files
- `packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/dev-mode.ts` — fix regex on line 128
- `packages/sidekick-plugin/skills/sidekick-config/SKILL.md` — no changes (source of truth for plugin)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
- After `pnpm sidekick dev-mode enable`, `.claude/skills/sidekick-config/SKILL.md` contains `pnpm sidekick` (not `npx @scotthamilton77/sidekick`)
- After `pnpm sidekick dev-mode disable`, `.claude/skills/sidekick-config/` directory is removed
- Source SKILL.md in `packages/sidekick-plugin/` still uses `npx @scotthamilton77/sidekick`

title: Fix publish:dist to auto-commit version bump and investigate double npm auth
description:
Two issues with the pnpm publish:dist workflow:

1. **Auto-commit version bump**: After a successful publish, the version-bumped files (package.json etc.) should be automatically committed. Currently they're left as dirty working tree changes.

2. **Double npm authentication prompt**: Publishing triggers two separate auth prompts — once for the publish with tag alpha, then again for updating the 'latest' tag. Investigate why this happens and fix if possible. The output shows:
   - First auth: 'Publishing to registry with tag alpha and public access'
   - Second auth: 'also updating latest tag'

This suggests the script is running two separate npm publish commands (one for alpha tag, one for latest), each requiring its own auth. Should be a single publish or reuse the auth session.

Acceptance criteria:
- On successful publish, version-bumped files are auto-committed with semantic commit message
- Only one npm auth prompt per publish (or explain why two are unavoidable)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: DaemonClient.start() spawns even when daemon already running with matching version
description: Test 'should skip spawn when daemon running with matching version' in daemon-client.test.ts fails: spawn is called once when it should not be called at all. The daemon's version-matching skip logic is broken — it spawns a new daemon process even when an existing one reports a matching version. Pre-existing bug, not caused by vitest upgrade.

title: Hooks silently fail when setup not run - no user feedback
description:
## Problem

When sidekick is installed as a plugin (via --plugin-dir or npm package) but `sidekick setup` has never been run:

1. Hooks execute and attempt to start daemon
2. Daemon starts but fails on first LLM operation (ProviderError: no API key)
3. CLI treats this as "daemon unavailable" and gracefully degrades
4. Hook returns `{}` with exit 0
5. **User sees nothing** - no error, no warning, no indication sidekick isn't working

This is worse than dev-mode where at least the statusline shows a warning. With plugin-only install, there's no statusline hook configured, so the user has zero feedback.

## Evidence

From `/Users/scott/src/projects/agents-config/.sidekick/logs/`:
- sidekick.log: `"error":"[-32603] OpenRouter requires apiKey..."` at WARN level
- sidekickd.log: `"error":{"name":"ProviderError","provider":"openrouter"}` at ERROR level
- But hook still returns success with no output to Claude Code

## Solution Design

Hooks should check setup status **before** attempting daemon/IPC operations:

### Setup States → Behavior

| State | Log Level | Action |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| `not-run` | INFO | Return additionalContext + userMessage, skip daemon |
| `partial` | WARN | Return additionalContext + userMessage, skip daemon |
| `unhealthy` | WARN | Return additionalContext + userMessage, skip daemon |
| `healthy` | - | Normal hook execution |

### Implementation

1. In CLI hook command (`packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/hook.ts`), check setup status early
2. If not healthy: log appropriately, return response with additionalContext + userMessage, exit 0
3. Do NOT start daemon or attempt IPC in degraded mode
4. Statusline hook excluded (operates differently with its own setup checks)

### Messages

**additionalContext**: Explain to Claude that sidekick features won't work until configured
**userMessage**: Tell user to run `sidekick setup` to configure

Both should be returned on **every hook invocation** - intentionally noisy until user configures or removes plugin.
notes:
## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Hook command checks `SetupStatusService.getSetupState()` before IPC
- [ ] `not-run` state: logs at INFO level, returns additionalContext + userMessage
- [ ] `partial` state: logs at WARN level, returns additionalContext + userMessage  
- [ ] `unhealthy` state: logs at WARN level, returns additionalContext + userMessage
- [ ] `healthy` state: proceeds with normal daemon/IPC flow
- [ ] Daemon is NOT started when setup not healthy
- [ ] No ProviderError logged (because we never attempt LLM calls)
- [ ] Statusline hook excluded from this check (has its own handling)
- [ ] Tests verify degraded mode behavior
- [ ] Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Code review and simplify statusline setup integration
description:
Review and simplify the statusline setup status integration code changes.

## Files to review
- `packages/feature-statusline/src/statusline-service.ts`
- `packages/feature-statusline/src/types.ts`
- `packages/feature-statusline/src/__tests__/statusline.test.ts`

## Code Review Findings

### Important Issues (Fix)

1. **Mock over-engineered** (`statusline.test.ts:180-204`)
   - MockSetupStatusService implements 6 methods but MinimalSetupStatusService only requires 2
   - Remove dead methods: `getApiKeyHealth`, `isHealthy`, `getUserStatus`, `getProjectStatus`
   - Add `implements MinimalSetupStatusService` to mock for type safety

2. **Only checks one API key** (`statusline-service.ts:421`)
   - `unhealthy` case only checks OPENROUTER_API_KEY, ignoring OPENAI_API_KEY issues
   - Consider checking both keys or dynamically determining which is problematic

### Nice-to-Have Improvements

3. **Use imported ApiKeyName type** (`statusline-service.ts:28`)
   - Replace inline union `'OPENROUTER_API_KEY' | 'OPENAI_API_KEY'` with imported `ApiKeyName` from `@sidekick/core`

4. **Extract empty view model as constant** (`statusline-service.ts:448-471`)
   - buildMinimalViewModel() creates a static shape with only `summary` varying
   - Extract base object as EMPTY_STATUSLINE_VIEWMODEL constant

5. **Specify which key is invalid** (`statusline-service.ts:429`)
   - "API key invalid" message doesn't specify WHICH key
   - Change to "OPENROUTER_API_KEY invalid..." or equivalent

### What's Good (No Changes Needed)
- MinimalSetupStatusService interface follows ISP well
- Warning messages are clear and actionable
- `setup_warning` DisplayMode fits naturally with existing pattern
- Well-documented JSDoc comments

## Acceptance criteria
Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. All findings above addressed.

title: Code review and simplify setup wizard implementation
description:
Code review and simplification of the setup wizard implementation.

## Files reviewed:
- packages/types/src/setup-status.ts
- packages/sidekick-core/src/setup-status-service.ts
- packages/sidekick-core/src/__tests__/setup-status-service.test.ts
- packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/setup/index.ts
- packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/setup/prompts.ts
- packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/setup/validate-api-key.ts
- packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/setup.ts
- packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/__tests__/setup.test.ts

## Code Review Findings

### Important Issues (Fix)

1. **Dead code: validate-api-key.ts is completely unused** (setup/validate-api-key.ts)
   - Entire file (49 lines) is dead code, duplicates logic in @sidekick/shared-providers
   - Uses WRONG endpoint (openrouter.ai/api/v1/models vs /api/v1/key)
   - If accidentally imported, validation always passes. Delete this file.

2. **Empty API key accepted and written to .env** (setup/index.ts:496)
   - promptInput returns empty string if user hits Enter
   - Empty string gets validated (network call with empty bearer token) then written to .env
   - Add empty-input guard before calling validate

3. **Regex in readKeyFromEnvFile doesnt strip quotes or trailing whitespace** (setup-status-service.ts:305)
   - Regex ^KEY=(.+)$ captures raw match including wrapping quotes
   - Users with quoted .env values or Windows line endings get misleading invalid key errors
   - Add trim() and quote-stripping

4. **runDoctorCheck sequentially validates API keys** (setup-status-service.ts:865-942)
   - Loops through keys with for...of + await, doubling latency
   - Both keys could be detected in parallel with Promise.all

### Nice-to-Have Improvements

1. **Re-detect after writing makes redundant network call** (setup/index.ts:514)
   - detectAllApiKeys re-validates key we just validated
   - Pass skipValidation: true or construct result from known state

2. **runDoctor always spawns Claude session for liveness check** (setup/index.ts:852)
   - Every sidekick setup --check spawns full Claude process (30s timeout)
   - Consider making liveness check opt-in with --live flag

3. **Test comments describe old values** (setup-status-service.test.ts:186,693,698,707,722,729,741,764)
   - Several test descriptions reference old schema values that dont match assertions
   - e.g. returns not-setup when assertion checks none

4. **writePersonaConfig has unused _projectDir parameter** (setup/index.ts:248)
   - YAGNI. Remove if no plans for project-level persona configs

5. **Type assertion soup in promptSelect return values** (setup/index.ts:325-328,491-494,526-527)
   - Every promptSelect call ends with as cast
   - Make promptSelect generic: promptSelect<T extends string>

6. **getUserStatus throws on malformed JSON but returns null on invalid schema** (setup-status-service.ts:196-211)
   - Asymmetric error handling. Add comment explaining design choice at minimum.

7. **autoConfig ask preference stored but never acted upon** (setup/index.ts:526-530)
   - ask and manual both map to false. Either implement ask behavior or remove option.

### Whats Good (No Changes Needed)
- Clean separation of concerns with step functions and shared WizardContext
- Dual-scope architecture (UserSetupStatus/ProjectSetupStatus) well-designed
- Dev-mode guardrails prevent overwriting dev statusline commands
- Bidirectional cache correction in doctor check
- Comprehensive test coverage including bug regression tests
- Defensive spawn management with safeResolve pattern
- Barrel file pattern keeps imports clean
- PromptContext abstraction makes prompts testable

## Acceptance criteria
Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. All findings above addressed.

title: Capture the sidekick-config skill as part of the plugin
Fix race condition: concurrent handle creation in SessionLogWriter.write()
description: Two concurrent fire-and-forget logEvent() calls for the same sessionId/logFile can both pass the !entry check, both await mkdir(), and the second write() overwrites the first in the Map — orphaning the first WriteStream's file descriptor. Fix: store a pending-creation Promise<HandleEntry> sentinel in the Map immediately before the await mkdir(), so concurrent callers wait on it rather than creating duplicate streams. File: packages/sidekick-core/src/session-log-writer.ts:62-91. Found by both RALF reviewers (plan review bead e9d4).

ASSESSMENT COMPLETE (2026-04-12)

Branch worktree-agent-aaab9092 contains 5 commits after the docs commit.

TASK STATUS:
- Task 1 (PathResolver.sessionLogsDir): COMPLETE — path-resolver.ts + test
- Task 2 (SessionLogWriter class): COMPLETE — session-log-writer.ts + tests, exported via index.ts + structured-logging.ts

EPIC DESCRIPTION DRIFT: Epic says 'All 6 implementation tasks done' — INCORRECT. Tasks 4 and 6 are missing.

BINDINGSESSIONID WIRING (phuu task): bindSessionId() does not exist as a named function. The wiring concern is whether event.context.sessionId is populated when logEvent fires. Daemon already passes sessionId in context for session-scoped events (eventReceived, hookInvocationHandled, etc.). Lifecycle events use EMPTY_CONTEXT (sessionId='') and will be skipped by the writer correctly. Once Task 4 is implemented, the wiring should work. phuu task should add integration test confirming this.

GAP SUMMARY: 2 implementation tasks incomplete (4, 6). Plan tasks blocked on these are: phuu (depends on daemon wiring from Task 4), koy1 (E2E), v4s6 (stress tests), g81i/r6tw/e5hr (completion gate), 1y0v/4v6d (delivery).

title: Subagent-Aware Sidekick
description: Make Sidekick work flawlessly on multi-agent, multi-worktree sessions, with UI properly supporting and representing these aspects. Empirical ground truth from claude-hook-probe experiment (25 questions, 429 events) documented in docs/CLAUDE-CODE-SUBAGENT-HOOK-BEHAVIOR.md.

title: fix(ui): stale closure in TranscriptRow memo comparator skips onPairNavigate
description: Copilot review on PR #169 identified a real bug: TranscriptRow's custom memo comparator doesn't include onPairNavigate, so when detailPanel.expanded changes, rows keep stale scrollToIndex closures that won't dispatch SELECT_TRANSCRIPT_LINE. Fix by stabilizing the callback via a ref and/or updating the comparator.
close_reason: Closed

title: Doctor doesn't update stale user-setup-status.json API key entries
description: When doctor runs, getEffectiveApiKeyHealth reads the project-level file first. If the project file has correct 'healthy' status, cacheNeedsUpdate is false and the stale user file is never touched. Even when the fix block IS entered, user status is only written when it doesn't exist — never updated when stale. Two bugs: (1) doctor's cache check is satisfied by project file so user file is ignored, (2) the user file write path only creates, never updates.
close_reason: Closed

Also: analysisInFlight Map not replaced with CoalescingGuard, duplicate prompt constants, double getMetrics() call, WHAT-comments, redundant logger param.
close_reason: PR #149 merged. Completed periodic path migration: generateSnarkyMessage/generateResumeMessage reduced to thin wrappers, analysisInFlight replaced with CoalescingGuard, fixed stale countdown closure bug found by Copilot. Net -252 LOC.

title: Human-required complexity fixes — architectural judgment needed
description: Beads in this epic require human-in-the-loop for design decisions. These involve decomposing god objects with shared mutable state, choosing abstraction boundaries, and designing dependency injection patterns. Wrong choices here create worse problems than the original — circular dependency webs, leaky abstractions, or awkward DI graphs. Agents can handle mechanical parts (moving code, updating imports) but the architect must make the design decisions. These are multi-session efforts. DO NOT let an agent solo these.
notes:
## Plan & Rationale

These beads require human architectural judgment. They decompose god objects with shared mutable state, requiring decisions about abstraction boundaries, ownership of state, and dependency injection patterns. Wrong choices create worse problems than the originals — circular dependency webs, leaky abstractions, or awkward DI graphs that nobody wants to maintain.

### Why human is crucial:

**pt2 — Daemon decomposition (1,952 LOC, 12 responsibilities, 76 private fields)**
The constructor is 187 lines of wiring. Extracting IpcRouter, PersonaLifecycleManager, SessionLifecycleManager, TimerManager, LogMetricsTracker, and DaemonContextFactory sounds clean on paper, but: if IpcRouter needs PersonaLifecycleManager needs SessionLifecycleManager needs LogMetricsTracker... you get a circular dependency web instead of a god class. The extraction ORDER matters. The DI pattern matters. An agent will create 6 modules that technically compile but have an awkward dependency graph.

**nq6 — TranscriptService decomposition (1,836 LOC, 19 private state fields)**
Many fields are shared between concerns. toolUseIdToName is used by both ExcerptBuilder and MetricsEngine. excerptBuffer feeds into both FileWatcher and ExcerptBuilder. The circular buffer, byte offset tracking, and metrics accumulation are deeply interleaved. Extracting clean modules from tangled state is a DESIGN problem, not a refactoring problem.

**osf — Session-summary deduplication**
The snarky/resume generation is ~80% identical between periodic and on-demand paths, but the 20% difference is where the design judgment lives. The periodic path gets transcript from the analysis flow; on-demand fetches its own. Periodic emits events; on-demand returns results. Finding the right shared abstraction that handles both without becoming a leaky mess — that is architecture, not mechanics.

### Children:
- **pt2** (P1 epic) — Decompose Daemon god class
- **nq6** (P1 epic) — Decompose TranscriptService god object
- **osf** (P1 refactor) — Deduplicate + decompose session-summary generation pipeline

### Execution Protocol:
1. Scott participates in design session (plan mode) to decide extraction boundaries and DI patterns
2. Agent handles mechanical extraction under Scott's direction
3. Scott reviews intermediate state after each extraction (not all at once)
4. Multi-session efforts — do NOT attempt in a single conversation
5. Standard PR workflow applies for each extraction step

### Memory Protocol:
**On claim**: Run bd memories extraction, bd memories pattern, bd memories decomposition, and bd memories god-class to pull ALL learnings from Phase 1 (xp4) and Phase 2 (zyk). These beads exist specifically to benefit from accumulated extraction experience. Pay special attention to: barrel re-export patterns (from 1bl, xvc), DI patterns (from n4f), cross-package gotchas (from xvc, x43).

**On close**: Use bd remember to capture the decomposition patterns, DI decisions, and shared-state resolution strategies. These are the highest-value learnings in the entire complexity remediation effort. Include: what extraction order worked, how shared state was resolved, what the coordinator pattern looks like.

**After all 3 epics complete**: Run bd memories extraction and bd memories pattern to review ALL accumulated memories from the complexity analysis effort. Delete stale entries with bd forget — patterns specific to a single extraction that won't generalize, gotchas that were one-time issues, interim learnings superseded by later experience. Keep: reusable extraction patterns, DI conventions, cross-package refactoring protocols. The goal is a lean set of durable wisdom, not a pile of session notes.

title: Medium-risk complexity fixes — agent-assisted, review carefully
description: Beads in this epic involve bounded design decisions or high blast radius. Agents can take the first pass in a worktree, but PRs must be reviewed carefully before merging. Copilot review via /wait-for-pr-comments will catch some issues, but won't catch bad abstraction choices. Key risks: choosing which behavior to keep when unifying duplicates, missing call site updates across many files, getting dependency injection patterns right, and blast radius on cross-package type changes.
notes:
## Plan & Rationale

These beads involve bounded design decisions or high blast radius. Agents can take the first pass in a worktree, but PRs need careful human review before merging. Copilot via /wait-for-pr-comments catches regressions, but won't catch bad abstraction choices.

### Key risks by bead:
- **h8a** (flush bug) — Subtle buffer lifecycle semantics; edge cases in pre-upgrade replay timing
- **xvc** (types split) — Mechanical move but entire monorepo imports from types; one missing re-export breaks everything
- **1dq** (CLI utilities) — Unifying 3 different prompt implementations requires choosing which behavior to keep
- **n4f** (SetupStatusService) — DoctorEngine reconciliation logic mutates shared state across branches; DI must be right
- **x43** (reminders DRY) — Each extraction is small but touches 10+ files; easy to miss a call site
- **ef0** (statusline) — discoverPreviousResumeMessage refactoring needs injection pattern decision
- **c8w** (UI decomposition) — TranscriptLine split is mechanical; DetailPanel component extraction needs prop interface design; hover fix needs React perf knowledge
- **mh2** (test gaps critical) — Blocked by pt2/q2e. Writing good tests requires knowing what edge cases matter, not just what compiles
- **cne** (test gaps UI) — React Testing Library setup + understanding component rendering contracts

### Children:
- **h8a** (P0 bug) — Fix flush() silent log loss
- **xvc** (P1 refactor) — Split events.ts + state.ts by domain in types
- **1dq** (P1 refactor) — Extract shared CLI utilities
- **n4f** (P1 refactor) — Decompose SetupStatusService
- **x43** (P1 refactor) — Feature-reminders DRY + observability
- **ef0** (P1 refactor) — Statusline service cleanup
- **c8w** (P1 refactor) — UI component decomposition
- **mh2** (P1 tests) — Test gaps: daemon + CLI critical paths
- **cne** (P1 tests) — Test gaps: UI frontend components

### Execution Protocol:
Agent takes first pass autonomously in worktree. Opens PR. Runs /wait-for-pr-comments. THEN Scott reviews the PR diff before merge. For beads touching many files (x43, xvc), run full monorepo test suite, not just the target package.

### Memory Protocol:
**On claim**: Run bd memories extraction and bd memories pattern to check for learnings from Phase 1 (xp4) beads and any previously completed Phase 2 beads. Apply relevant patterns — especially barrel re-export patterns from 1bl, god-file extraction patterns from q2e.

**On close**: Use bd remember to capture reusable patterns, gotchas, or surprises. Focus on: DI patterns used, cross-package impact lessons, files that needed updating beyond the obvious scope. Use bd close with a --reason that summarizes the key insight.

**After all 3 epics complete**: Run bd memories extraction and bd memories pattern to review accumulated memories. Delete stale or superseded entries with bd forget. Keep only patterns that remain useful for ongoing maintenance.
close_reason: All 9 child beads completed: 1 P0 bug fixed (h8a), 6 refactors (ef0, c8w, n4f, x43, xvc, 1dq), 2 test suites (mh2, cne). 9 PRs merged to main. 19 Copilot comments addressed across 3 waves. ~610 new tests. Multiple god-files decomposed.

title: Agent-safe complexity fixes — autonomous execution
description: Beads in this epic are mechanical refactors and bug fixes that can be executed autonomously by agents without human oversight. Each has clear boundaries, well-defined acceptance criteria, existing test coverage to validate against, and no architectural judgment calls needed. Agents should claim and execute these in isolated worktrees. Trust the PR review process — /wait-for-pr-comments will catch regressions.
notes:
## Plan & Rationale

These beads are mechanical refactors and bug fixes that can be executed autonomously by agents without human oversight. Each has clear boundaries, well-defined acceptance criteria, existing test coverage to validate against, and no architectural judgment calls needed. Agents should claim and execute these in isolated worktrees. Trust the PR review process — /wait-for-pr-comments will catch regressions.

### Why agent-safe:
- Move code to new file + update imports (no behavior change)
- Add try/catch around existing code (additive, no restructuring)
- Write Zod schema tests (mechanical: valid → passes, invalid → fails)
- Delete dead code (verified via grep, git history preserves)
- Swap data structure (string key → nested Map, existing tests validate)

### Children:
- **s20** (P0 bug) — Add try/catch in stageToolsForFiles inner loop
- **odz** (P0 bug) — Fix promptYesNo hang on stdin close
- **9sm** (P1 bug) — Replace latency buffer string key with nested Map
- **1bl** (P1 refactor) — Extract LogEvents namespace from structured-logging.ts
- **q2e** (P1 refactor) — Extract doctor/scripted from setup/index.ts
- **b5m** (P1 tests) — Zod schema runtime validation tests
- **bei** (P1 chore) — Delete .archive/ + investigate mock-data.ts

### Execution Protocol:
Each bead runs independently in its own worktree. No sequencing required — all can run in parallel. Standard workflow: worktree → branch → PR → build/typecheck/lint → tests → /wait-for-pr-comments → address feedback → close bead.

### Memory Protocol:
**On claim**: Run bd memories extraction and bd memories pattern to check for learnings from previously completed beads. Apply any relevant patterns.

**On close**: Use bd remember to capture any reusable patterns, gotchas, or surprises discovered during the work. Include: what worked, what was tricky, what a future agent should know. Use bd close with a --reason that summarizes the key insight.

**After all 3 epics complete**: Run bd memories extraction and bd memories pattern to review accumulated memories. Delete stale or superseded entries with bd forget. Keep only patterns that remain useful for ongoing maintenance.
close_reason: All 7 child beads completed: 3 bugs fixed (s20, odz, 9sm), 2 refactors (1bl, q2e), 1 test suite (b5m), 1 chore (bei). 7 PRs merged to main. 10 Copilot comments addressed. Simplify pass run on all 7.

title: Test gaps: UI frontend components have near-zero test coverage
description: The UI server API layer is well-tested (7 test files, good coverage). The React frontend has near-zero component tests: TranscriptLine.tsx (634 LOC, multi-branch rendering, zero tests), Transcript.tsx (306 LOC, zero tests), SessionSelector.tsx (256 LOC, zero tests), useNavigation.ts (190 LOC, core state machine, zero tests), DetailPanel.tsx (229 LOC, zero tests), SubagentTranscript.tsx (165 LOC, zero tests). Need to establish UI test infrastructure (React Testing Library or similar) and add tests for the most complex components. buildViewModel token-selection branches also need dedicated tests after statusline cleanup extracts resolveEffectiveTokens.
notes:
## Acceptance Criteria
- UI test infrastructure established (React Testing Library or equivalent, configured in vitest)
- useNavigation reducer tested: all 14 action types, state transitions, edge cases
- TranscriptLine rendering branches tested: at minimum compaction, user-prompt, assistant, tool_use, tool_result, sidekick-event line types
- SessionSelector tested: date grouping logic (isToday, isYesterday, isThisWeek, isThisMonth), expand/collapse
- Transcript filter logic tested: category filtering, search filtering
- classifyTranscriptLine utility tested
- resolveEffectiveTokens tested (after statusline cleanup ef0 extracts it): all 4 token resolution branches
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Workflow Requirements
1. All work MUST be done in an isolated git worktree (isolation: worktree on Agent tool)
2. Create a feature branch (test/ prefix) and open a PR to main — NEVER commit directly to main
3. Before claiming done, run: pnpm build && pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint
4. Run: pnpm --filter @sidekick/ui test
5. Invoke the `simplify` Skill tool on changed files — address all findings
6. Commit fix-ups from simplify, then push and create PR to main using gh pr create
7. After PR is created, invoke /wait-for-pr-comments to monitor Copilot review
8. Address ALL review feedback from Copilot — do not close with unresolved comments
9. Only after review completes or times out AND all fixes are applied can the bead be closed

## Memory Protocol (MANDATORY — not optional)
**On claim**: Run `bd memories` and review these specific memories:
- `xp4-coverage-threshold-on-extraction` — UI components have near-zero coverage; establishing infrastructure is step 1
- `xp4-simplify-finds-real-issues` — simplify is mandatory even on test-only PRs

**Before close (HARD REQUIREMENT)**: Run `bd remember "cne: [key insight]"` capturing what was learned. Then `bd close` with `--reason`. Skipping this is a workflow violation.
close_reason: UI frontend test coverage established. 174 new tests across 7 files covering navigation reducer, transcript rendering, session date grouping, filter logic, and utility functions. Pure functions extracted for testability. PR #144.

title: [bug] Latency buffer key split(':') corrupts metrics for model names containing colons
description: In instrumented-llm-provider.ts, the latency buffer is keyed by template literal provider:model and later split with key.split(':'). A model name containing ':' (e.g. some provider namespacing) produces incorrect provider/model splits, corrupting percentile computation. Fix: replace string-keyed buffer with Map<string, Map<string, number[]>> to avoid the fragile split. Also extract getOrCreateModelMetrics helper to DRY the duplicated guard blocks in recordSuccess/recordFailure. File: packages/sidekick-core/src/instrumented-llm-provider.ts
notes:
## Acceptance Criteria
- Latency buffer replaced with Map<string, Map<string, number[]>> (nested map by provider then model) — eliminates fragile split(':') parsing
- getOrCreateModelMetrics helper extracts duplicated guard blocks from recordSuccess/recordFailure
- computePercentiles updated to iterate nested maps instead of splitting string keys
- Unit test covers: model name containing ':', normal model name, percentile computation with nested map
- No regression in existing LLM metrics tests
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Workflow Requirements
1. All work MUST be done in an isolated git worktree (isolation: worktree on Agent tool)
2. Create a feature branch (fix/ prefix) and open a PR to main — NEVER commit directly to main
3. Before claiming done, run: pnpm build && pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint
4. Run: pnpm --filter @sidekick/core test -- --testPathPattern instrumented-llm
5. After PR is created, invoke /wait-for-pr-comments to monitor Copilot review
6. Address ALL review feedback from Copilot — do not close with unresolved comments
7. Only after review completes or times out AND all fixes are applied can the bead be closed

## Memory Protocol
**On claim**: Run `bd memories` to check for learnings from previously completed beads in this epic (xp4). Apply any relevant patterns.
**On close**: Use `bd remember` to capture any reusable patterns, gotchas, or surprises discovered during the work. Include: what worked, what was tricky, what a future agent should know. Use `bd close` with a `--reason` that summarizes the key insight.
close_reason: Replaced Map<string, number[]> latency buffer with Map<string, Map<string, number[]>> nested map. Extracted getOrCreateModelMetrics and getOrCreateLatencyBuffer helpers. Updated computePercentiles to iterate nested maps. 3 new tests cover colon-containing model names. PR #129.

title: Investigate stop hook firing verification reminders after subagent already verified
description: Main agent sometimes receives a stop hook reminder to check stuff even when the subagent that did the work already performed verification. This suggests the stop hook logic doesn't account for verification done by subagents. Session 039f3d2f-759b-424a-b550-ceb5cf622cd9 contains evidence of this behavior. Investigate the stop hook conditions and determine how to detect that verification was already completed by a delegated subagent.
notes:
## Worktree Daemon Isolation (2026-04-11)

Concrete mechanism confirmed via session log analysis (discord-agent-scaffold sessions fea2ddf3, c4097745):

When a subagent runs with isolation: 'worktree', Claude Code routes its hooks to a DIFFERENT daemon instance keyed to the worktree's cwd as the project dir. This means:

1. Worktree subagent runs pnpm build/typecheck → worktree daemon sees it, unstages VC reminders for THAT session
2. Main session's Stop hook fires → main session's daemon has SEPARATE staged VC state, has never seen the worktree verification
3. Main daemon fires VC reminder. Main agent knows subagent verified (Agent tool result is in context). Both are correct — they're talking to different daemons.

The ToolCall/ToolResult events for the subagent's Bash commands live in a SEPARATE JSONL under the worktree's project dir. The main daemon never processes them.

This is the worktree-specific hard version of the general problem. State isolation fix (7qet) must account for cross-daemon state, not just cross-agent state within one daemon.

title: Strip command runners (uv run, npx, etc.) before tool pattern matching
description: The tool-pattern-matcher uses anchored first-token matching, which fails when commands are invoked through runners like 'uv run mypy', 'npx jest', 'poetry run pytest', etc. The first token is the runner (e.g. 'uv') instead of the tool (e.g. 'mypy'), so patterns never match. Need to add a configurable runner-stripping layer that preprocesses command segments before pattern matching. Should cover runners across all supported language ecosystems (Node: npx/bunx/pnpx, Python: uv run/poetry run/pipx run, etc.).
close_reason: Implemented in PR #127. Added command_runners config, unanchored matching when runner prefix detected, handler wiring, YAML defaults. All tests pass.

title: Refine decision:recorded usage — remove non-decisions, add missing decision sites
description: Two related fixes: (1) Remove decision:recorded emissions for actions that always happen unconditionally (e.g. 'UserPrompt forces immediate analysis' — there's no choice being made, so it's not a decision). Only log decision:recorded when there's an actual branching choice. (2) Add decision:recorded events for staging/unstaging of tool-specific VC reminders based on thresholds — these ARE real decisions with conditions but aren't currently logged. Discovered from pkj rename work.
close_reason: PR #121 opened: moved DecisionEvents to @sidekick/types, removed 2 non-decisions, added 5 new positive-only decision sites in feature-reminders

title: Rename DecisionRecordedPayload.detail to subsystem, make title required
description: Design flaw: detail field is misnamed — it's always 'session-summary analysis', functioning as a subsystem tag, not a detail. When we needed a human-readable label, we had to add title as an optional field because detail was squatting on that semantic role. Fix: rename detail→subsystem (or feature), make title required. Update all 5 emission sites, the event factory, the type definition, and the UI transcript parser. No backward compat needed per project constraints, but the transcript-api parser should handle old log entries with detail instead of subsystem gracefully.
close_reason: PR #117 opened: renamed detail→subsystem, made title required, wired into UI parser

title: [bug] LEDs not activating when reminders are staged
description: During manual UI testing, the LED indicators in the gutter column do not activate/illuminate when reminders are being staged. The LED system should reflect staging activity — when a reminder is staged, the corresponding LED should light up. Investigate whether: 1) The daemon is emitting the LED state change events, 2) The UI is receiving but not rendering them, 3) The LED mapping for staging events is missing.
close_reason: False positive — LEDs are activating correctly, staging events found during manual testing.

title: [bug] Slash commands show as green tool bubbles with wrong filtering
description: Slash commands (any <command-*> user transcript line) show in the timeline as green tool-call bubbles and filter under 'system'. This is inconsistent — they are user actions. Desired behavior for ALL slash commands (/clear, /compact, /help, etc.): 1) Filter as part of 'chat' (not 'system'), 2) Use user chat bubble color and indentation, 3) Keep the tool-call icon as a visual indicator that the message triggered a command. The detail pane already shows these as user messages — the timeline should match.
notes:
## Playwright Verification

**Before fix (confirm bug):**
1. Launch UI, navigate to a session transcript containing slash commands (e.g. /clear, /compact, or any <command-*> line)
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm slash commands appear as green tool-call bubbles
3. Click the 'system' filter — confirm slash commands are included (wrong category)
4. Click the 'chat' filter — confirm slash commands are NOT included (should be here)
5. Click a slash command bubble — confirm detail pane shows it as user-message (inconsistent with bubble style)
6. Test with multiple different slash commands to confirm the bug is systemic

**After fix (confirm resolution):**
1. Same session transcript
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm ALL slash commands (<command-*> lines) appear with user chat bubble color/indentation
3. Confirm tool-call icon is still present on each slash command bubble
4. Click the 'chat' filter — confirm slash commands ARE included
5. Click the 'system' filter — confirm slash commands are NOT included
6. Click a slash command bubble — confirm detail pane still shows user-message (now consistent)
7. Test with multiple different slash commands to confirm the fix is systemic
close_reason: PR #111 merged. Slash commands now filter as conversation, render with blue user-chat bubbles and Terminal icon.

title: Improve decision event phrasing in timeline
description: Decision events in the timeline are awkwardly phrased: 'decision calling' and 'decision skipped' don't read well. Change format to: 'Decision: {concise decision title}' where the title describes WHAT the decision is (not the reason). Example: 'Decision: Skip bulk processing' not 'Decision: calling BulkProcessingComplete - analyzing full transcript'. In the details pane, show both the decision title and the reason/rationale.
notes:
## Playwright Verification

**Before fix (confirm bug):**
1. Launch UI, navigate to a session transcript that contains decision events
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm decision events show awkward phrasing like 'decision calling' or 'decision skipped'
3. Click a decision bubble — snapshot the detail pane content

**After fix (confirm resolution):**
1. Same session transcript
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm decision events now show 'Decision: {concise title}' format
3. Click a decision bubble — confirm detail pane shows both the decision title and the reason/rationale
4. Verify multiple decision types render correctly (calling, skipped, etc.)
close_reason: PR #113 - Added title field to DecisionRecordedPayload, populated at all 5 emission sites. Timeline now shows 'Decision: Skip/Run/Defer session analysis' instead of 'Decision: skipped/calling'. Detail pane shows title, category badge, and reasoning.

title: [bug] Unstaged events shown when nothing was actually unstaged
description: After the user's first prompt, a bunch of 'unstaged' events appear in the timeline. The unstage code fires legitimately (it has to run to check), but the UI should only show unstage events when a reminder was ACTUALLY unstaged — not when the unstage ran but found nothing to unstage. IMPORTANT: Maintain debuggability. The events should still be logged for debugging purposes (daemon logs), but the timeline should filter them out or mark them differently when no actual unstaging occurred. Discuss approach before implementing — don't want to lose the ability to debug staging/unstaging issues.
notes:
## Playwright Verification

**Before fix (confirm bug):**
1. Launch UI, start or navigate to a fresh session transcript (after first prompt)
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm spurious 'unstaged' events appear even though nothing was actually unstaged
3. Count the number of unstaged events visible

**After fix (confirm resolution):**
1. Same scenario — fresh session after first prompt
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm no unstaged events appear when nothing was actually unstaged
3. Navigate to a session where a reminder WAS genuinely unstaged — confirm those unstaged events still appear correctly
4. Check daemon logs to confirm the unstage check events are still being logged for debuggability
close_reason: Fixed: deleteReminder returns boolean, unstaged events only emitted when file actually deleted. PR #116 merged.

title: Hook events: capture inputs on start, return value on finish, show hook name
description: Hook events in the timeline should be more informative. Requirements: 1) Hook start event should capture and display the inputs/arguments passed to the hook. 2) Hook finish event should capture and display the return value given back to Claude Code. 3) The hook name should be visible in the details pane (currently it is not). All three should be visible in the detail panel when clicking on a hook event.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #119

title: [bug] Summary analysis finishes before intent/session_title are reported
description: In the timeline, the summary analysis operation shows as complete BEFORE the intent and session_title events appear. But intent and session_title are outputs of that analysis. The ordering should show the analysis completing and THEN the derived values (intent, session_title) being emitted, or the derived values should appear as part of the analysis finish event.
notes:
## Playwright Verification

**Before fix (confirm bug):**
1. Launch UI, navigate to a session transcript that contains summary analysis with intent/session_title
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm the summary analysis 'complete' event appears BEFORE the intent and session_title events
3. Note the ordering/timestamps of these events

**After fix (confirm resolution):**
1. Same session transcript (or trigger a new analysis)
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm intent and session_title events appear as part of or immediately after the analysis completion
3. Verify the temporal ordering is now logical: analysis runs → analysis completes → derived values emitted (or derived values shown as part of completion)
close_reason: Fixed in PR #120

title: Rename 'Summary Analysis' to 'Session Analysis' or 'Transcript Analysis'
description: The LLM operation labeled 'Summary Analysis' in the timeline is misleading. It should be called either 'Session Analysis' or 'Transcript Analysis' to better describe what it does (analyzing the transcript to extract intent, session title, etc.).
notes:
## Playwright Verification

**Before fix (confirm bug):**
1. Launch UI, navigate to a session transcript that contains the LLM analysis operation
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm it shows 'Summary Analysis' as the label

**After fix (confirm resolution):**
1. Same session transcript
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm the label now reads 'Session Analysis' or 'Transcript Analysis' (whichever is chosen)
3. Verify both the start and finish events use the new label consistently
4. Search the timeline for any remaining occurrences of 'Summary Analysis' — confirm none exist
close_reason: Fixed in PR #120

title: Standardize LLM operation start/finish labels
description: LLM operation start/finish pairs are inconsistent across event types. Example: Snarky Message start says 'Snarky Message...' and finish says 'Snarky Message - {message}'. Summary Analysis start/finish shows no data at all. Standardize all LLM operations to use consistent format: start label = '{operation name}' (no ellipsis), finish label = '{operation name}: {result}'. Audit all LLM operation types (snarky, resume, summary analysis, etc.) and make them uniform.
close_reason: PR #122 standardizes all LLM operation labels to consistent format

title: [bug] BulkProcessingComplete decision appears after /clear
description: After starting a session with /clear, the timeline shows 'Decision: calling BulkProcessingComplete - analyzing full transcript'. This is unexpected — a cleared session should not trigger bulk processing of a full transcript. Investigate why this fires after /clear. Note: existing bead 7ri tracks adding BulkProcessingComplete start/finish lifecycle events — this bead is about the unexpected triggering.
notes:
## Playwright Verification

**Before fix (confirm bug):**
1. Launch UI, start a fresh session that begins with /clear
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm 'Decision: calling BulkProcessingComplete - analyzing full transcript' appears unexpectedly after /clear

**After fix (confirm resolution):**
1. Same scenario — fresh session starting with /clear
2. Snapshot the timeline — confirm no BulkProcessingComplete decision event appears after /clear
3. Navigate to a session with a genuine bulk processing scenario — confirm the BulkProcessingComplete decision still fires correctly when appropriate
4. Verify /clear properly resets whatever state triggers the bulk processing check
close_reason: Fixed in PR #112 — one-shot guard prevents spurious BulkProcessingComplete after /clear, lifecycle events added

title: [bug] Duplicate 'Staged ...' bubbles in timeline
description: During manual UI testing, duplicate 'Staged ...' bubbles appear in the timeline. Investigate whether the daemon is logging staging events twice, or if bulk processing is causing double-emission. Check daemon event emission code paths for staging reminders.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #118. Added existsSync check in stageReminder to suppress duplicate reminder:staged events on overwrite.

title: Fix 'sidekick ui' command to use Vite dev server instead of missing production.js
description: The 'pnpm sidekick ui' command fails because it tries to spawn server/production.js, which was archived in commit 4ecf918 (v2 prototype, 2026-03-07). The production.ts was part of the old Phase 7 implementation and got swept into .archive/ with the rest of the old server code. The UI has been working fine via 'pnpm --filter @sidekick/ui dev' (Vite dev server with api-plugin middleware). Fix: update the ui command handler to launch Vite dev server instead of the missing production server script.
close_reason: Fixed: ui command now spawns Vite dev server instead of missing production.js. Merged fix/ui-vite-dev-server into main.

title: Add structured logging to silent catch blocks in sessions-api
description: listProjects and listSessions have bare catch {} blocks that silently swallow all errors — file permission issues, disk I/O failures, JSON parse errors. Should add structured logging (matching project's pino approach) so these failures are visible during development.
close_reason: PR #107 merged to main

title: Parallelize sequential file reads in listSessions
description: listSessions reads session-summary.json and session-persona.json sequentially per session in a for loop. For N sessions this is 2N sequential I/O operations. Should use Promise.all to read both files per session concurrently, and potentially Promise.allSettled across sessions.
close_reason: PRs #108 and #109 merged to main

title: Add change detection guard to useSessions setState calls
description: useSessions hook calls setProjects() unconditionally on every fetch cycle, even when data hasn't changed. This creates new array references that trigger unnecessary re-renders in all consuming components. Should compare new data with previous state and skip setState when equivalent.
close_reason: PRs #108 and #109 merged to main

title: Visually distinguish branch from worktree in statusline
description: The statusline currently shows branch and worktree info but they are not visually distinct. Add styling/formatting to make it immediately clear which is the branch name and which is the worktree path, improving at-a-glance readability.
close_reason: branchWT token implemented with split colorization, worktreeOrBranch removed, all tests pass

title: UI/UX round-3 feedback: hooks, single-line events, unified filters, scroll sync
description: 8 feedback items: hook events, single-line sidekick events, center-justified positioning, transcript→timeline scroll, unified filters, color families, summary naming, statusline content

title: Investigate statusline context graph color thresholds — hardcoded limit causing false red at 15%
