description: Fix all Copilot review comments: ErrorOccurredEvent type union unsoundness, payload source collision, sessionId sentinel, dropped context fields, duplicate PersonaEvents factories, and improve error-event test coverage
close_reason: PR #63 created with all fixes

title: Persona reverts to original after /clear when switched in-session
description: If a persona is switched mid-session (via persona set), then the user does /clear, the original persona gets restored instead of keeping the new one. Likely a cache invalidation issue or race condition with other sessions reading stale persona state.
close_reason: Fixed in a prior session

title: Feature: project registry in ~/.sidekick/ for UI discovery
description: The UI needs to discover which projects have been 'sidekicked'. On startup, it reads ~/.sidekick/ to find a registry of known projects (paths). This registry is populated when sidekick is first used in a project. The UI uses this to build the project selector before the user picks a session. Discovered during API layer architecture brainstorming (sidekick-4385facb).
close_reason: Project registry already implemented — ProjectRegistryService with register/list/prune and 5s heartbeat

title: Define canonical UIEventType union in @sidekick/types
description: Add UIEventType union, EventVisibility type, and per-event payload interfaces to packages/types/src/events.ts. This is the foundation all other unified event contract changes depend on. See IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md §2.4 for the complete event table and §2.2 for naming convention. Acceptance: Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
close_reason: PR #55 merged — canonical UIEventType union, 31 payload interfaces, visibility map, CanonicalEvent generic all in @sidekick/types

title: Bug: reminders:throttle-restage crashes with null stageReminder
description: The reminders:throttle-restage transcript handler crashes repeatedly with 'Cannot read properties of null (reading stageReminder)'. 553 error-level entries in sidekickd.1.log. Fires on both UserPrompt and AssistantMessage events. Recent commits 4aefacd and 26ae87b claimed to fix this handler but the error persists in logs.
close_reason: Stale build: dist/ was not rebuilt after fix commits 26ae87b and 4aefacd merged. Daemon was running old code with staging: null. Rebuild + daemon restart resolved all 553 errors.

title: Fix throttle-restage handler closing over null staging context
description: The reminders:throttle-restage handler captures daemonCtx at registration time (line 67 of stage-default-user-prompt.ts), but the registrationContext is created with staging: null (line 1770 of daemon.ts). setContext() later creates a NEW object rather than mutating the original, so the handler forever sees staging: null. This causes 'Cannot read properties of null (reading stageReminder)' on every UserPrompt and AssistantMessage event — 495 errors in daemon log.
close_reason: Merged PR #44. Handlers now use invocation-time ctx parameter instead of closed-over registration context with staging: null.

title: Spec: Data contracts — schema definitions for UI-backend communication
description:
Produce Section 3 of IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md: Data contracts.

## Context
Data flows from .sidekick/ files through a Node.js backend to the React frontend. The backend reads files and serves them as JSON; the frontend renders them. Currently StateSnapshot uses Record<string,unknown> for all fields — a type safety regression.

## Input Documents
- PHASE2-AUDIT §1.1 (18 state types defined in @sidekick/types)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §2.4 (state file inventory: 7 files with schemas and update cadence)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §4.4 (recommends replacing Record<string,unknown> with strict types)
- REQUIREMENTS.md §4-5 (feature definitions that imply data needs)
- DO NOT reference MONITORING-UI.md (deprecated)

## Scope
Define TypeScript interfaces for every API response:
1. Session list (directory listing → typed response)
2. Log stream (NDJSON lines → typed parsed events)
3. Each state file from PHASE2-AUDIT §2.4 (daemon-status, transcript-metrics, session-summary, snarky-message, resume-message, log-metrics, compaction-history)
4. Staged reminders (per-hook directory → typed collection)
5. Updated StateSnapshot type replacing Record<string,unknown> with imports from @sidekick/types

## Acceptance Criteria
- TypeScript interface defined for every API endpoint's request and response
- All 18 state types from PHASE2-AUDIT §1.1 referenced by canonical @sidekick/types import path
- StateSnapshot fields use strict types, not Record<string,unknown>
- Zod schemas referenced where they exist (for runtime validation at the backend boundary)
close_reason: Section 3 Data Contracts spec complete. PR #49 opened. Passed two rounds of subagent review.

title: Spec: Event adapter mapping — logging events to UI timeline events
description:
Produce Section 2 of IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md: Event adapter mapping.

## Context
The codebase has 28+ logging event types (events.ts) but the UI only handles 16 SidekickEventTypes. There are naming mismatches (e.g., HandlerExecuted→EventProcessed) and structural mismatches (UI expects start/finish pairs but daemon logs single events). An archived event-adapter.ts pattern existed but is not in the current codebase.

## Input Documents
- PHASE2-AUDIT §1.1 (full logging event type inventory)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §1.2 (current 16 UI event types)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §2.5 (4 naming mismatches)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §4.1 (recommends Option C hybrid: logging events in log viewer panel, not main timeline)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §4.3 (recommends restoring archived event-adapter.ts pattern)
- REQUIREMENTS.md §4 F-3 (timeline event types), §5 G-8 (structured logging / log viewer)
- DO NOT reference MONITORING-UI.md (deprecated)

## Scope
Define:
1. Canonical mapping table: each logging event type → UI event type (or 'log viewer only')
2. Resolution for each naming mismatch in PHASE2-AUDIT §2.5
3. Strategy for start/finish pairs vs single events (synthesize pairs? change UI expectations?)
4. Which events appear on main timeline vs log viewer panel
5. Adapter module location and interface (in UI backend, not @sidekick/types)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete mapping table covering all 28+ logging event types
- Each of the 4 naming mismatches from PHASE2-AUDIT §2.5 has an explicit resolution
- Clear boundary defined: main timeline events vs log-viewer-only events
- Adapter interface defined (input type, output type, error handling)
close_reason: Section 2 written in IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md. Requirements backlog created as beads gw7, rtz, 6ua, 4fc, 5e5, rli, 0zv, 68p.

title: Spec: API layer architecture — how UI reads backend data
description:
Produce Section 1 of IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md: API layer architecture.

## Context
The UI needs to read .sidekick/ data files. The daemon is IPC-only (Unix socket, JSON-RPC 2.0) — there is no HTTP server. PHASE2-AUDIT §4.2 recommends Option C: file-based reads via Node.js backend (Vite dev proxy for dev, standalone Node server for production).

## Input Documents
- REQUIREMENTS.md §6 (file-based data source), §7 (no WebSocket)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §2.1-2.3 (no HTTP server exists, daemon architecture), §2.4 (state file inventory with update cadence), §4.2 (Option C recommendation)
- DO NOT reference MONITORING-UI.md (deprecated)

## Scope
Define:
1. Backend architecture (Vite proxy vs standalone Node server vs both)
2. File read strategy: which files, polling vs fs.watch, polling intervals
3. Error handling: missing files, locked files, corrupt JSON, log rotation mid-read
4. Scope resolution: how UI discovers .sidekick/ vs ~/.sidekick/ and presents both
5. API route design: REST endpoints the React frontend calls

## Acceptance Criteria
- Section written in IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md with architectural decision and rationale
- All state files from PHASE2-AUDIT §2.4 accounted for in the API surface
- Error cases enumerated with handling strategy
- Scope resolution (.sidekick/ vs ~/.sidekick/) explicitly addressed
close_reason: Section 4 written in IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md with all acceptance criteria met. PR #54 merged.

title: UI Implementation Spec: bridge requirements and audit to actionable implementation plan
description:
Consume REQUIREMENTS.md (Phase 1) + PHASE2-AUDIT.md (Phase 2) + v2 prototype learnings to produce a single implementation spec document (packages/sidekick-ui/docs/IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md) that tells a developer exactly how to wire the prototype to real data.

This replaces the original Phase 3 (UX Rethink) and Phase 4 (New Implementation Spec) from the sidekick-n4lx epic, both of which are now redundant as separate phases.

## Document Chain

MONITORING-UI.md (deprecated, historical context only — DO NOT use for implementation decisions)
  → REQUIREMENTS.md (approved requirements — the 'what')
  → PHASE2-AUDIT.md (gap analysis — the 'gap map')
  → IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md (this epic's output — the 'how')

Each child task produces a section of IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md document produced with all 6 sections
- Each section has clear data contracts, component mappings, or architectural decisions
- Document references REQUIREMENTS.md and PHASE2-AUDIT.md by section number (not MONITORING-UI.md)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Status bar context % not reflecting actual context usage
description: The context percentage shown in the status bar is no longer reflecting the actual context usage. The /context command shows accurate numbers but the statusline display is wrong.
close_reason: Fixed: added output_tokens to context calculation and widened baseline guard for incomplete cache data at session start

title: Create skill for persona creation/enrichment using session lessons
description:
## Summary

Create a new skill (or enhance the existing `sidekick-personas` skill) that codifies the persona creation and enrichment workflow discovered in this session. The skill should guide Claude through creating new personas or enriching existing ones with authentic, source-accurate quotes and vivid character profiles.

## Context / Lessons Learned

During bead sidekick-x8nc, we developed a proven 2-phase approach for persona enrichment:

### Phase 1: Research
- Web search for iconic character quotes from source material (movies, TV, books)
- Identify the character's defining traits, speech patterns, and catchphrases
- Collect 25-30 candidate quotes per character

### Phase 2: Edit
- Adapt quotes to coding/programming context (swap one key noun, keep the rest recognizable)
- Target counts: 18-20 statusline_empty_messages, 5-7 snarky_examples, 4-5 snarky_welcome_examples
- Theme: 200-400 characters with full name, source, role, vivid personality description, coding context
- personality_traits: 6-8 specific hyphenated compound traits (not generic adjectives)
- tone_traits: 5-6 speech-pattern descriptors (HOW they speak, not WHAT)
- Validate YAML after every edit
- Check for within-file duplicates (same quote in multiple sections)
- Check for cross-character contamination (quotes attributed to wrong character)
- Preserve llmProfile if present
- Behavioral directives in tone_traits (e.g., "no mechanical sounds") must be preserved

### Quality Gates
- YAML parse validation
- Count verification against targets
- Build passes (pnpm build)
- Persona tests pass (pnpm --filter @sidekick/core test -- 'persona')
- No within-file duplicate quotes across sections
- No misattributed quotes

### Gold Standard Example (theme)
Avasarala: "Chrisjen Avasarala, UN Undersecretary turned Secretary-General from The Expanse. The solar system's foul-mouthed political razor: sari-clad, cane-wielding, bullshit-intolerant genius who swears like she breathes and cuts through deception like a railgun. She'll call your code 'horseshit' to your face, then fix the architecture because humanity — or at least this goddamn project — depends on it."

## Implementation

1. Look at existing skills under `packages/sidekick-plugin/skills/` to understand the skill format and find the right place to add this (likely enhance `sidekick-personas` skill or create a new companion skill)
2. Use the `/writing-skills` skill to ensure proper skill structure and quality
3. Document the 2-phase approach, targets, quality gates, and gold standard examples
4. Include YAML validation commands and test commands
5. Sync to `.claude/skills/` per the dual-location requirement

## Acceptance Criteria
- Skill exists and is loadable by Claude Code
- Covers both new persona creation AND enrichment of existing personas
- Includes all target counts, quality gates, and validation steps
- References gold standard examples
- Synced between packages/sidekick-plugin/skills/ and .claude/skills/
close_reason: Skill created, tested, synced between packages/ and .claude/skills/

title: Audit token counting algorithm against actual LLM token usage
description: Investigate the current token counting/estimation algorithm in the codebase and compare its output against actual token counts from LLM API responses. Identify any discrepancies, measure accuracy, and determine if calibration is needed. Check both prompt and completion token estimates vs actuals.
close_reason: Audit complete. Findings: (1) 4:1 char heuristic is test/emulator-only, no production impact. (2) Transcript token extraction (TranscriptService) is healthy — millions of actual API tokens tracked per session. (3) Sidekick's own LLM metrics (InstrumentedLLMProvider) working correctly. (4) Context metrics CLI capture is broken — filed as sidekick-peba. Token counting is sound overall.

title: Modernize sidekick-ui: architecture alignment, UX rethink, and implementation spec
description:
## Overview

The sidekick-ui package was designed as a "Time Travel" debugging dashboard for observing Sidekick's hook system, daemon activity, and session state. An MVP was built with React 18 + Vite + TailwindCSS, featuring a cockpit layout (timeline rail, event stream, state inspector), time-travel replay, compaction-aware snapshots, and daemon health monitoring.

However, the broader Sidekick architecture has evolved significantly since the UI was designed. The UI needs to be brought into alignment, its UX goals reconsidered, and a fresh implementation spec written.

## Current State

**What exists (packages/sidekick-ui/):**
- React SPA with complete component library (Header, Timeline, Transcript, StateInspector, MetricsPanel, SystemHealth, etc.)
- Time-travel replay engine (TimeTravelStore) with NDJSON log parsing
- Event adapter, trace correlator, filter parser
- Compaction snapshot viewer with diff support
- Production HTTP server (Node native) with itty-router API
- 8 API endpoint handlers (logs, sessions, metrics, summaries, compaction, daemon status, staged reminders)
- CLI integration via `pnpm sidekick ui` command
- Design doc: packages/sidekick-ui/docs/MONITORING-UI.md (433 lines)

**What's broken or stale:**
- UI runs entirely on hardcoded mock data (src/data/mockData.ts) — API integration stubbed but untested
- Event schema in mock data may not match current @sidekick/types canonical types
- No end-to-end validation against real .sidekick/ log files or daemon output
- No virtual scrolling — large sessions (1000+ events) will lag
- Design doc predates several architecture changes (feature packages, staging pattern, provider abstraction, persona system)
- TailwindCSS 3.x (current ecosystem is on v4)
- React 18.2 with local TypeScript 5.2.2 (monorepo uses 5.9.3)

## Epic Scope

### Phase 1: Requirements Gathering
- Mine existing design docs (MONITORING-UI.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, flow.md) for original requirements and user stories
- Catalog all requirements from the original UI design: what problems was it solving, for whom, and why?
- Review current architecture (feature packages, staging pattern, provider abstraction, persona system) to infer new potential requirements
- Identify capabilities that now exist in the system but have no UI representation
- Present findings to user: validate which original requirements still hold, which are obsolete, and what new requirements emerge from the evolved architecture
- Produce a consolidated requirements document with user sign-off before proceeding

### Phase 2: Architecture Audit
- Inventory all @sidekick/types event types and compare against UI's type guards and event cards
- Audit API handlers against actual daemon/CLI log output format
- Identify new features (personas, reminders, session summaries, staged files) that need UI representation
- Document what the UI's design doc (MONITORING-UI.md) gets right vs. what's stale

### Phase 3: UX Rethink
- Reassess the primary user: who uses this UI and why? (debugging? monitoring? demos?)
- Evaluate whether the "time travel" paradigm is the right primary UX or if simpler views (live dashboard, session browser, config inspector) serve users better
- Consider progressive disclosure: what should be visible by default vs. on-demand?
- Evaluate tech stack: keep React+Vite+Tailwind or consider alternatives (e.g., terminal UI, simpler static page)?
- Design for the actual data volume and update frequency observed in real sessions

### Phase 4: New Implementation Spec
- Write a fresh spec that replaces MONITORING-UI.md
- Define clear feature tiers (must-have, nice-to-have, future)
- Specify data contracts between UI and API (aligned with current @sidekick/types)
- Define performance requirements (event count targets, polling intervals, rendering budget)
- Plan for scope handling (.sidekick/ vs ~/.sidekick/ cascade)

### Phase 5: Implementation (child beads)
- Align package dependencies with monorepo standards (TS 5.9.3, vitest 4.x, etc.)
- Implement against the new spec
- Wire real data through API handlers
- Add integration tests with actual log fixtures
- Performance optimization (virtual scrolling, incremental updates)

## Key References
- Current design doc: packages/sidekick-ui/docs/MONITORING-UI.md
- Architecture: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
- Event model: docs/design/flow.md
- Monorepo packages: packages/AGENTS.md
- CLI command: packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/ui.ts
- Production server: packages/sidekick-ui/server/production.ts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Requirements document with user sign-off
- Architecture audit document produced identifying all gaps
- UX goals document with clear user stories and design rationale
- New implementation spec replacing MONITORING-UI.md
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Scripted --auto-config=auto doesn't detect actual plugin scope
description: When running sidekick setup --auto-config=auto without --plugin-scope, the code defaults to 'user' instead of detecting the actual installed scope. This lets auto-configure be enabled even when the plugin is project-scoped. Fix: detect actual plugin scope from claude plugin list --json before validating.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement daemon kill-zombies and doctor zombie detection
description:
Add zombie daemon process detection and cleanup:

1. sidekick daemon kill-zombies: New subcommand that finds and kills sidekick daemon processes that are NOT tracked in the ~/.sidekick/daemons/ PID registry. These are orphaned/zombie processes from crashed sessions or unclean shutdowns. Should scan for node processes matching the daemon binary pattern and compare against registered PIDs.

2. Enhance sidekick doctor: Add a zombie check that detects unregistered daemon processes. In --fix mode, automatically kill zombies (equivalent to kill-zombies). Add 'zombies' to the DOCTOR_CHECK_NAMES list so it can be targeted with --only=zombies.

3. sidekick daemon kill-all already exists and kills all tracked daemons. kill-zombies complements it by targeting the untracked ones.

Acceptance criteria: Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. kill-zombies finds and terminates unregistered daemon processes. doctor reports zombie daemons. doctor --fix kills them.
close_reason: Implemented daemon kill-zombies and doctor zombie detection. 4 commits, 10 new tests, all passing.

title: Bug: uninstall command missing from --help text
description: In packages/sidekick-cli/src/cli.ts, the GLOBAL_HELP_TEXT (line ~332) lists all available commands but omits uninstall. The command is implemented and routed (line ~581) but users who run sidekick --help will not discover it. Fix: add 'uninstall [--force]     Remove sidekick (plugin, data, config)' to GLOBAL_HELP_TEXT.
close_reason: Added uninstall to --help text, added --help handler with all flags documented, added test coverage

title: Config change to enable persona injection mid-session does not trigger re-staging
description: When injectPersonaIntoClaude is changed from false to true mid-session, handleConfigChange reloads the config but never calls stagePersonaRemindersForSession for active sessions. The persona watcher only fires on session-persona.json changes, not config changes. Fix: detect persona injection config change in handleConfigChange and trigger re-staging for all active sessions.
close_reason: Config change detection added in handleConfigChange; restagePersonaRemindersForActiveSessions delegates to existing stagePersonaRemindersForSession. Both directions (enable/disable) handled. Tests passing.

title: Address code coverage gaps in 6 recently closed beads
description:
Unit test coverage gaps identified across 6 beads closed 2026-02-16. Add tests for edge cases not covered.

## sidekick-kqu (Bash git-status diffing)
- Concurrent Bash executions in same session (baseline race)
- Source code patterns config missing/malformed (fallback behavior)
- Git not available — handler behavior when getGitFileStatus returns []

## sidekick-jhx (Persona transition placeholders)
- State files corrupted/unreadable before transition
- Integration: regenerateMessagesForSession ordering guarantee

## sidekick-kt0 (Daemon health state)
- Statusline integration with failed daemon health (degraded display)
- daemonAvailable=false threading — skip-IPC path end-to-end
- Concurrent writes to daemon-health.json

## sidekick-32u (VC classifier text entries)
- Buffer wraparound at circular buffer boundary
- count=0 or negative count parameter
- Empty text content entries (type=text, content="")

## sidekick-0zlp (Doctor missing user setup)
- Corrupted JSON in user file (getUserStatus → null)
- Both user AND project files missing
- User missing + API key also unhealthy (multiple failure signals)

## sidekick-wh8d (Doctor --fix flag)
- --fix on already-healthy state (no-op, exit 0)
- --fix --only=api-keys (unfixable-only filter)
- Dev-mode active when fixing statusline (should skip)
- Idempotency — --fix twice → second run healthy

Acceptance criteria: Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. All gaps above have test cases.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audit all config settings for production readiness
description: Before declaring production-ready, audit every configuration setting and default value across all scopes (user/project/local). Includes but not limited to: debug mode flags, statusline defaults, API key handling, daemon settings, persona defaults, feature flags (features.yaml), setup-status.json schema, .env variable expectations, and any hardcoded defaults in the codebase. Verify each setting has correct defaults, is documented, and behaves consistently across install/uninstall/upgrade paths.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix uninstall: clean all three settings scopes (user/project/local)
description: Uninstall command only cleans two of three possible settings file locations. Install supports user (~/.claude/settings.json), project (.claude/settings.json), and local (.claude/settings.local.json) scopes, but uninstall maps project→settings.local.json and user→settings.json, completely missing .claude/settings.json. detectProjectScope() also only checks settings.local.json, not settings.json. Fix: detect and clean all three scope files during uninstall.

title: E2E Test: auto-configure new projects flow
description: Test the auto-configure opt-in flow end-to-end: (1) Run sidekick setup on a project and opt in to auto-configure new projects, (2) Navigate to a brand new project directory that has never seen sidekick, (3) Start claude in that new project, (4) Verify: Does sidekick auto-configure? Does setup-status.json get written? Do hooks fire? Does the persona appear? Does the statusline work? (5) Test edge cases: what if the plugin is installed but auto-config is off? What if auto-config is on but the plugin isn't installed? Acceptance criteria: Document observed behavior for each scenario. File bugs for anything broken.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix plugin liveness check cwd for project-scoped plugins
description: detectPluginFromCLI() and detectPluginLiveness() in setup-status-service.ts spawn the claude CLI without specifying cwd. This means project-scoped plugins/hooks are not loaded when the doctor check runs from a different directory. Fix: add cwd: this.projectDir to both spawn calls.

title: Scripted setup (CLI flags) doesn't write setup-status.json
description:
runScripted() in setup/index.ts configures statusline, gitignore, personas, API keys, and auto-config preferences correctly, but never writes setup-status.json to the project .sidekick/ directory. The interactive runWizard() path calls writeStatusFiles() at line 702 but runScripted() has no equivalent call. This means hooks always see "project not configured" after non-interactive setup.

Fix: runScripted should write a project setup-status.json reflecting what was configured.

Acceptance criteria: Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. Running `npx @scotthamilton77/sidekick setup --statusline-scope=project --personas` writes .sidekick/setup-status.json.

title: fix: devMode flag lost on setup-status.json overwrite paths
description: Multiple code paths write setup-status.json via writeProjectStatus() without preserving the devMode field. autoConfigureProject() is the primary culprit (fires on SessionStart), runDoctorCheck() is a sleeper risk. Fix: (1) Replace --force with --forceDevMode for explicit semantics, (2) auto-correct devMode when --forceDevMode seen but flag is off, (3) preserve devMode in all overwrite paths.

title: Inject active persona into Claude Code's own system prompt
description:
When a persona is activated, inject persona awareness into Claude Code's responses via the UserPromptSubmit hook's additionalContext. Since personas are session-scoped (not global), CLAUDE.md is not viable — must use the hook staging system.

## Prerequisite: Multi-Reminder Concatenation

The current consumption handler (consumption-handler-factory.ts) only consumes the single highest-priority reminder per hook invocation. This feature requires concatenating additionalContext from ALL staged reminders for a given hook, sorted by priority (highest first). Changes needed:

- Modify consumption handler to iterate all staged reminders, not just reminders[0]
- Concatenate additionalContext fields in priority order (highest first)
- Preserve existing persistence semantics: non-persistent reminders consumed/renamed after delivery, persistent ones stay
- userMessage and blocking fields: TBD whether to merge or use highest-priority winner (likely highest-priority wins for these)

## New Staged Reminders (UserPromptSubmit)

Up to 3 reminders can be staged, concatenated by priority into additionalContext:

1. **Existing persistent reminder** (current behavior, highest priority) — no changes needed
2. **PersonaChanged reminder** (non-persistent, one-shot) — staged when persona changes mid-session via CLI. Informs Claude the persona has changed and what the new persona is. Fires once, then auto-clears.
3. **RememberYourPersona reminder** (persistent) — steady-state reminder of the active persona's voice/personality. Lowest priority of the three.

Priority ordering: default → PersonaChanged → RememberYourPersona

## Persona Change Detection

Users can change personas mid-session via `pnpm sidekick persona set <id> --session-id=<id>`. The system must detect this change and:
- Stage a one-shot PersonaChanged reminder
- Update the persistent RememberYourPersona reminder with the new persona
- When persona is cleared, remove both persona-related reminders

## Configuration

- Add config option (e.g. `persona.injectIntoClaude`) defaulting to true
- When false, neither persona reminder is staged regardless of active persona

## Acceptance Criteria

- Consumption handler concatenates additionalContext from all staged reminders by priority
- Activating a persona causes Claude to adopt that persona's voice via additionalContext
- Changing persona mid-session stages a one-shot PersonaChanged notification
- RememberYourPersona persists across turns until persona is cleared
- Config flag defaults to true; setting false suppresses persona reminders
- Clearing persona removes both persona-related reminders
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
close_reason: All 6 acceptance criteria verified: multi-reminder concatenation, persona injection via additionalContext, one-shot PersonaChanged, persistent RememberYourPersona, config flag, cleanup on clear. Build/typecheck/tests (208/208) all pass.

title: CLI ignores unrecognized switches instead of failing fast
description:
Running `pnpm sidekick setup --check-only` (an unrecognized switch) silently ignores the switch and proceeds with the command, which in this case wrote to the project.

**Expected behavior:**
1. Fail fast with error: "unrecognized switch '--check-only'"
2. Show the help output for the command (e.g., `sidekick setup --help`)
3. Exit with non-zero code

**Scope:** This should apply to ALL commands, not just setup. Any unrecognized switch/option should trigger this behavior.

**Impact:** High - user intent is ignored, potentially destructive (setup modified project when user wanted read-only check)

**Note:** The correct switch for doctor mode is `--doctor` not `--check-only`

title: dev-mode clean-all fails to delete .sidekick/setup-status.json
description: The `pnpm sidekick dev-mode clean-all` command does not remove `.sidekick/setup-status.json` from the project directory. This file should be cleaned up along with other dev-mode artifacts.

title: Investigate using async hooks for sidekick
description: Research and evaluate using Claude Code's async hooks instead of synchronous hooks for sidekick operations. Async hooks may provide better performance and user experience by not blocking Claude Code's main loop.
close_reason: 5 of 7 hooks must return data to Claude Code (context injection, permission decisions, block/allow). Only SessionEnd and PreCompact are fire-and-forget. The staging pattern already provides async benefits — daemon does heavy work in background, CLI hooks just read staged files and return immediately. Switching to async would break core functionality with no meaningful gain.

title: Enhance doctor with plugin status detection and conflict resolution
description:
Enhance the doctor command and hook execution to properly detect plugin installation modes and resolve conflicts when both dev-mode and official plugin are active.

**Problem**: When both dev-mode AND --plugin-dir (or official plugin) are active, hooks fire twice because Claude Code sees them as different hook configurations.

**Solution**:

1. **Doctor detects and records** `pluginStatus` in `setup-status.json`:
   - `dev-mode` - dev-sidekick hooks detected, no official plugin
   - `installed-user` / `installed-project` / `installed-both` - official plugin
   - `plugin-dir` - neither detected but liveness passes (test mode)
   - `conflict` - dev-mode AND official plugin both detected
   - `not-installed` - nothing detected, liveness fails

2. **Dev-mode scripts pass `--force`** when invoking `sidekick hook`

3. **Hook execution checks conflict**:
   - Read `pluginStatus` from setup-status.json
   - If `conflict` AND `--force` not passed → bail early (let dev-mode win)
   - If `conflict` AND `--force` passed → execute (this is dev-mode calling)

4. **Statusline also checks** the same logic

**Implementation tasks**:
- Add `pluginStatus` to types: `packages/types/src/setup-status.ts`
- Add `claude plugin list` parsing: `packages/sidekick-core/src/setup-status-service.ts`
- Update doctor to detect & write status
- Add `--force` flag to hook command: `packages/sidekick-cli/src/commands/hook.ts`
- Update dev-mode scripts to pass `--force`: `scripts/dev-sidekick/*`
- Check conflict in hook execution
- Check conflict in statusline: `packages/feature-statusline/src/statusline-service.ts`

**Reference**: docs/plans/sidekick-setup-e2e-test-plan.md - discovered during Scenario 6 testing
acceptance_criteria: Doctor correctly detects and reports pluginStatus. setup-status.json contains pluginStatus field. Dev-mode scripts pass --force. Hook execution bails early on conflict without --force. No duplicate hook execution when in conflict state. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes: **Testing note**: After implementation, must run `npm publish` in packages/sidekick-dist before testing in plugin mode (--plugin-dir or official install). Dev-mode testing works with local build only.

title: Doctor command should check actual config state, not just cache
description: Doctor reports statusline and API keys as not configured even when they are. Root cause: SetupStatusService reads from cache files (setup-status.json) not actual config files. Fix: Doctor should probe actual config state, compare with cache, update cache if mismatched, and report corrections to user.

title: Post-plugin-install setup wizard
description:
CONTEXT:
Claude Code plugins cannot provide statusLine config - only hooks. Users need a setup step after installing the sidekick plugin.

FEATURE: `sidekick setup` command

FLOW:
1. **Statusline scope**: Ask user if they want statusline at user level (~/.claude/settings.json) or project level (.claude/settings.json)

2. **API key configuration**:
   - Check if OPENROUTER_API_KEY (or other provider keys) are in environment
   - If not found, ask: 'Create .sidekick/.env file for API keys?'
   - If yes, ask for scope: user (~/.sidekick/.env) or project (.sidekick/.env)
   - Ask user to provide key now OR tell them to add it manually later

3. **Completion message**:
   - Summarize what was configured
   - Tell user: 'Start Claude Code and ask Claude to help customize sidekick using the /sidekick-config skill'

REQUIREMENTS:
- Interactive prompts (inquirer-style or simple stdin)
- Idempotent - safe to run multiple times
- Detect existing config and offer to update/skip
- Support --user and --project flags to skip prompts

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `sidekick setup` walks through interactive setup
- `sidekick setup --user` non-interactive user-scope setup
- `sidekick setup --project` non-interactive project-scope setup
- Statusline appears after setup + Claude restart
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: BUG: claude running sidekick gets stuck

We need to either fail-fast when we think we're in sandbox mode (maybe by detecting the tmp folder location?) or else find a way for commands that don't require the daemon to ensure those don't get stuck.

title: Package install needs to modify ,gitignore

title: Bundle daemon.js separately for npm distribution
description:
PROBLEM:
In the bundled npm package (@scotthamilton77/sidekick), the daemon cannot start because:
1. `require.resolve('@sidekick/daemon/package.json')` fails (package doesn't exist separately)
2. Fallback path `../../sidekick-daemon/dist/index.js` doesn't exist in npm install location

This blocks all plugin tests (pr7.2, pr7.3, pr7.4).

FIX:
Bundle daemon as separate file alongside bin.js:
- sidekick-dist outputs both `dist/bin.js` and `dist/daemon.js`
- Update daemon-client.ts to resolve `daemon.js` as sibling file in bundled context

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `pnpm run bundle` in sidekick-dist produces both bin.js and daemon.js
- Daemon starts successfully when invoked via npx @scotthamilton77/sidekick
- Dev-mode still works (existing workspace resolution)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Implement --version CLI flag

Add --version/-v flag to sidekick CLI that displays the current version.

REQUIREMENTS:

- Should read version from package.json at build time (esbuild define/replace) or embed it during the bundle step
- Consider: sidekick-dist bundles everything, so the version should come from sidekick-dist/package.json

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

- `sidekick -v` outputs version number

title: Files written to ~/.sidekick/ instead of project .sidekick/
description: After removing the --scope CLI parameter, sidekick is writing logs, state, stage, and metrics to ~/.sidekick/ (user scope) instead of project-local .sidekick/ folder. Only daemon PIDs should go to ~/.sidekick/daemons/. Statusline works but no project-local activity observed.

title: Plugin hooks.json missing --scope and --project-dir parameters
description:
The plugin's hooks.json commands don't pass --scope and --project-dir parameters that the dev-hooks scripts pass.

Dev-hooks use:
  hook session-start --scope=project --project-dir="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

Plugin hooks.json uses:
  npx @scotthamilton77/sidekick hook session-start

Missing parameters:
1. --scope=user (for user-scoped plugin install)
2. --project-dir="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"

Impact:
- Without --scope, dual-install detection may cause hooks to exit early (cli.ts:212-214)
- Without --project-dir, CLI relies on env var which may not be reliable

Fix: Update all hook commands in packages/sidekick-plugin/hooks/hooks.json to include both parameters.

Discovered during Phase 11 plugin testing.
acceptance_criteria: All hook commands in hooks.json include --scope=user and --project-dir parameters. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: inject-stop.ts missing toolCount in metrics causes vc-unverified.set to fail
description:
inject-stop.ts has two bugs causing ZodError on vc-unverified.set:

1. Line 77: Fallback missing toolCount
   `const metrics = reminder.stagedAt ?? { turnCount: 0, toolsThisTurn: 0 }`
   Should be: `{ turnCount: 0, toolsThisTurn: 0, toolCount: 0 }`

2. Lines 85-88: metrics object only passes turnCount and toolsThisTurn
   Should also include: `toolCount: metrics.toolCount`

Same issue in onConsume handler at lines 126-133.

Error from logs:
```
ZodError: Invalid input: expected number, received undefined
path: ["setAt", "toolCount"]
```

This causes the first Write tool to not trigger the VC reminder because state isn't persisted.

title: Bundle assets in npm package for distribution
description:
The getDefaultAssetsDir() function in packages/sidekick-core/src/assets.ts uses relative paths from __dirname to find assets/sidekick/. This works in development but fails for npm installs because:

1. assets/sidekick/ is at monorepo root, not in any package
2. Package files fields only include ["dist"]
3. When users npm i -g @sidekick/cli, assets won't be present

Solution approach:
Assets should be accessed "in situ" from wherever sidekick is installed - NO copying to separate locations. This means:
1. Include assets in package.json files array so they ship with the npm package
2. Update getDefaultAssetsDir() to resolve to the correct location when running from an installed npm package (vs monorepo dev mode)

Acceptance criteria:
- npm pack of @sidekick/cli includes all required assets in the tarball
- getDefaultAssetsDir() resolves correctly after global npm install
- npx @sidekick/cli hook session-start works without monorepo context
- Assets accessed in-place from installed location (no copy step)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: TranscriptService crashes on large transcripts (>536MB)
description: When transcript files exceed V8's max string length (~536MB), TranscriptServiceImpl.processTranscriptFile crashes with 'Cannot create a string longer than 0x1fffffe8 characters' during readFileSync. Root cause: session f759532d had a 638MB transcript file. Daemon crashes and becomes unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: IPC layer detects oversized payloads early and fails with clear error. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: VC conditional staging is flaky
description:
The model's eval of a stop prompt (is it a completion boast, question, what?) seems flakey, and I'm seeing it NOT get staged for things like "Here's what I did.... but now PAUSING for input: ... 

See session e200e812-6713-4914-a898-370eebe3b755

title: Subagent support: detection, hook/reminder inheritance, and daemon behavior
description: Investigate how subagents work with sidekick's hook system. Key questions: (1) Can we detect subagent connections to parent agents? Relevant for analytics (SubagentStart/SubagentStop events). (2) Do subagents inherit our hooks and reminders? If so, are we accidentally spamming them with persona injections, stuck-loop reminders, or other session-scoped content? (3) How should sidekick behave when subagents are running — should hooks fire, should reminders be staged, should the daemon track them as separate sessions? Investigate Claude Code's subagent lifecycle and determine what sidekick needs to handle correctly.
notes:
## Empirical Answers (2026-04-05)

All three original questions answered by claude-hook-probe experiment. Full reference: docs/CLAUDE-CODE-SUBAGENT-HOOK-BEHAVIOR.md

### Q1: Can we detect subagent connections?
YES. SubagentStart/SubagentStop fire reliably (13/13). PreToolUse/PostToolUse inside subagents carry agent_id and agent_type fields. Detect subagent events by presence of agent_id.

### Q2: Do subagents inherit hooks and reminders?
YES. All registered hooks fire inside subagents. CLAUDE.md is visible to subagents. Sidekick IS currently spamming subagents with persona/reminder context because the schemas strip agent_id so there's no way to filter.

### Q3: How should sidekick behave with subagents?
- Accept agent_id/agent_type in Zod schemas (currently stripped)
- Register SubagentStart/SubagentStop hooks
- Key state on (session_id, agent_id) — concurrent hooks interleave
- Suppress most reminders for subagent events
- Track verification tool runs across agents
- Use SubagentStart additionalContext for intentional context injection

### Implementation scope expanded
This bead is now the foundation task for epic claude-code-sidekick-woi7 (Subagent-Aware Sidekick). Scope: Zod schema changes, CLI parsing, IPC dispatch, SubagentStart/SubagentStop registration.
close_reason: Closed

title: Resilient to bad JSON responses from LLMs
description: Handle malformed JSON responses from LLM providers gracefully
close_reason: Already resilient: all JSON.parse sites have try/catch with graceful fallbacks (raw content, null+warning, default results). Zod validation, markdown stripping, and FallbackProvider add additional layers. No crash path exists for bad LLM JSON.

title: Log rotation and default log level
description: Implement log rotation and change default log level to info
close_reason: Log rotation via pino-roll implemented. BufferedRotatingStream wired into createLogManager and createContextLogger. Config schema updated with rotation defaults (10MB/5 files). CLI and daemon call sites updated. Code review fixes applied.

title: Handle multiple sidekick hook installations gracefully
description: When sidekick hooks are installed in multiple scopes (user + project), all instances execute sequentially causing duplicative execution and odd side effects. Need smarter runtime coordination to detect and handle this - e.g., first hook sets an env var/marker that subsequent hooks check to skip execution.
acceptance_criteria: Sidekick hooks detect when another instance has already run for this event. Only one instance executes per hook event. No duplicative side effects when both user and project hooks are installed.
close_reason: Claude Code handles hook deduplication natively via $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR env var. No sidekick-side work needed.

title: Add comprehensive --help support to sidekick CLI
description:
Add --help support to all sidekick CLI commands:
- sidekick --help (list all commands with descriptions)

- sidekick daemon --help

Should also support -h shorthand for all commands.

title: Refactor CLI persona and sessions commands
description:
Refactor the CLI persona and sessions commands to function as follows:

**Commands:** (add --json to return a structured response)
- `persona list` - list the available persona ids
- `persona set {persona-id} --session-id={session-id}` - change that session's selected persona
- `persona clear --session-id={session-id}` - clear that session's selected persona

title: Add sidekick alias script under scripts/
description: Create a sidekick script or alias in the scripts/ folder that executes 'node packages/sidekick-cli/dist/bin.js'. This provides a convenient shorthand for running the CLI during development.

title: Strengthen persona influence in snarky/resume prompt templates
description:
The current prompts inject persona metadata (name, theme, personality, tone) but don't explicitly instruct the LLM to adopt the persona's voice. The persona block is just dumped without guidance on how to use it.

Changes needed:
1. Add explicit instruction in snarky-message.prompt.txt: 'Adopt this persona's voice and mannerisms when generating the comment'
2. Add explicit instruction in resume-message.prompt.txt: 'Generate output as this character would speak'
3. Consider adding persona-specific guidance like 'Match the tone and vocabulary demonstrated in the examples'
4. Potentially reference the theme more explicitly (e.g., 'You ARE this character')
5. Review and test with multiple personas to ensure strong voice differentiation
acceptance_criteria: Prompts explicitly instruct LLM to adopt persona voice. Generated output shows clear persona differentiation. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Move snarky/resume examples to persona YAML files and update prompt templates
description:
The embedded examples in snarky-message.prompt.txt and resume-message.prompt.txt are generic sidekick-style quips that pre-date the persona system. They should be moved to the persona YAML files (like statusline_empty_messages already are) so each persona has voice-appropriate examples.

Changes needed:
1. Add snarky_examples and resume_examples arrays to each persona YAML (skippy.yaml, ripley.yaml, etc.)
2. Create a 'disabled' persona profile (or defaults section) with neutral/generic examples for when personas are disabled
3. Update prompt templates to use {{persona_snarky_examples}} and {{persona_resume_examples}} placeholders
4. Update buildPersonaContext() in update-summary.ts to include these new fields
5. Handle the disabled case gracefully - either use the disabled profile examples or fall back to generic ones
acceptance_criteria: Persona YAMLs contain voice-appropriate examples. Prompt templates reference persona examples. Disabled persona has appropriate neutral examples. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Phase 9: Refactoring & Architecture Improvements
description: Comprehensive refactoring to improve code quality, test coverage, and architectural consistency. See docs/ROADMAP.md for details.

title: Guard spawnClaudeCli against hook recursion via SIDEKICK_SUBPROCESS env var
description:
## Why This Exists

spawnClaudeCli() spawns child claude -p processes with no env var guard. Sidekick hooks fire in those subprocess sessions, creating a potential recursion chain: LLM call → claude -p → Stop/UserPromptSubmit hooks → reminder check → another LLM call → infinite loop. No guard exists today.

Discovered while investigating --bare (claude-code-sidekick-dm0i): --bare would prevent hook firing but breaks OAuth auth. The right fix is an env-var guard instead.

## What Needs to Be Done

1. Add SIDEKICK_SUBPROCESS=1 to the env of all spawnClaudeCli() invocations (packages/shared-providers/src/claude-cli-spawn.ts — pass it in the spawn() env option)
2. In Sidekick hook handlers that trigger LLM calls (reminder hooks, persona hooks), check process.env.SIDEKICK_SUBPROCESS and short-circuit to a no-op if set
3. Add test coverage for the guard

## Acceptance Criteria

Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. Spawned claude -p processes have SIDEKICK_SUBPROCESS=1 in env. LLM-triggering hooks no-op when SIDEKICK_SUBPROCESS=1 is set.
close_reason: Merged in PR #178 (commit 54a97e35). SIDEKICK_SUBPROCESS=1 guard set on spawnClaudeCli; short-circuits handleUnifiedHookCommand with defense-in-depth inner guard in handleHookCommand. Copilot feedback addressed in 7eed5ecf.

title: Install sidekick CLI via Claude Code 'Plugin executables on PATH' feature
description:
Claude Code plugins can expose executables on PATH automatically. Sidekick currently installs its CLI via a bespoke setup step. Migrate to the plugin-provided PATH mechanism so the 'sidekick' command is available without custom install logic when the plugin is loaded.

Investigate: how the feature wires executables, naming/shim requirements, interaction with pnpm/npx, and whether dev-mode vs published plugin paths need different handling. Retire the current setup-time install once parity is reached.

title: Investigate new Claude Code hooks: PreCompact, PostCompact, StopFailure, CwdChanged, FileChanged, and conditional 'if' field
description:
Claude Code added several new hook types in March-April 2026 that Sidekick does not yet exercise:

- PreCompact (can block compaction via exit code 2 or {"decision":"block"})
- PostCompact (fires after compaction)
- StopFailure (fires on API error turn end)
- CwdChanged (fires on working directory change)
- FileChanged (fires on file change; useful for direnv-style flows)
- Conditional 'if' field on hooks using permission-rule syntax

Investigate each hook's payload, timing, and failure semantics. Identify which ones Sidekick should adopt (e.g. PostCompact for context-recovery reminders, StopFailure for resilience, conditional 'if' to scope hooks) and file follow-up feature beads per adoption candidate.

title: Leverage UserPromptSubmit hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle to set session title
description: Claude Code now supports setting the session title from a UserPromptSubmit hook via hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle. Wire Sidekick's UserPromptSubmit hook to emit a meaningful session title so sessions are easier to identify in the UI and in session listings.

title: Add refreshInterval configuration to statusline
description: Allow users to configure how often the sidekick statusline refreshes. Today the refresh cadence is not user-configurable. Expose a refreshInterval setting so operators can tune polling/render frequency for performance or responsiveness.

title: Fix mixed-session timeline history loss for sessions spanning deployment boundary
description: Timeline API parseTimelineEvents() uses exclusive-OR: if per-session logs exist, it reads ONLY from per-session files and skips aggregate entirely. A session that started before feature rollout (events in aggregate) and continued after (events in per-session files) will show only post-rollout events. Fix: merge both sources for sessions where per-session logs exist, deduplicating by timestamp+type. File: packages/sidekick-ui/server/timeline-api.ts:268-285. Found by RALF plan review (e9d4).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #176 (fix/session-log-writer-post-ship-bugs)

title: Fix YAML rotation defaults vs design intent: 10MB/5 files vs planned 2MB/2 files
description: The design spec says aggregate logs should use 2MB max / 2 rotated files (4MB total cap). But assets/sidekick/defaults/core.defaults.yaml ships maxSizeBytes: 10485760 (10MB) and maxFiles: 5. The fallback ?? 2_097_152 in daemon.ts and runtime.ts is dead code — config service always loads defaults first. DECISION: change core.defaults.yaml to 2MB/2 files to match design intent. Also remove the now-truly-dead ?? 2_097_152 fallback values or update to match the YAML defaults.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #176 (fix/session-log-writer-post-ship-bugs)

title: JSDoc and inline documentation cleanup for SessionLogWriter
description: Add or complete JSDoc on all public methods of SessionLogWriter (write, closeSession, close). Document the LRU eviction policy and idle timer behavior inline. Check if ARCHITECTURE.md or docs/design/ references the logging subsystem — if so, add a note about per-session log files. This is a P2 polish task; do not let it block the completion gate.
acceptance_criteria: All public SessionLogWriter methods have JSDoc. Architecture docs updated if applicable. Build passes.
close_reason: Completed as part of PR #176 delivery. Full completion gate ran: code-review, simplify, verify-checklist, Copilot review addressed.

title: Investigate --bare switch for claude -p execution
description:
## Why This Exists

The `claude -p` (print/pipe) mode is used for non-interactive Claude Code invocations. The `--bare` flag exists but its behavior, limitations, and applicability to Sidekick's use cases are not well understood.

## What Needs to Be Done

Research and document what `--bare` does and doesn't do when combined with `claude -p`:

- What output does `--bare` suppress or modify?
- Does it affect JSON output, status lines, tool call rendering, or exit codes?
- Are there scenarios where `--bare` would improve Sidekick's subprocess invocations?
- Are there scenarios where `--bare` would break things (e.g., structured output parsing)?
- Does it interact with `--output-format` (json, stream-json, text)?
- Test against actual Sidekick hook invocations to see if signal-to-noise improves

## Acceptance Criteria

- Documented findings in a bead note or design doc
- Clear recommendation: adopt, ignore, or partially adopt `--bare` in Sidekick invocations
- If applicable, a follow-up bead created for any implementation changes
notes:
## --bare Investigation Findings

### What --bare Actually Does
From `claude --help`:
- Skips: hooks, LSP, plugin sync, attribution, auto-memory, background prefetches, keychain reads, CLAUDE.md auto-discovery
- Sets: CLAUDE_CODE_SIMPLE=1
- Auth: STRICTLY requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or apiKeyHelper via --settings. OAuth and keychain are NEVER read.
- Skills: still resolve via /skill-name
- Context: must be explicitly provided (--system-prompt, --add-dir, --mcp-config, etc.)

### Sidekick's Two Invocation Sites

**1. AnthropicCliProvider.complete()** (packages/shared-providers/src/providers/anthropic-cli.ts)
Args: -p --no-session-persistence --setting-sources local --model X --output-format json
- Benefit of --bare: faster startup, no hook recursion (real risk today — no guard exists)
- RISK: Breaks OAuth auth. Most users auth via OAuth, not ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Adding --bare here would silently break LLM calls for those users.
- --output-format json still works with --bare (no issue there)
- Verdict: DO NOT ADD --bare

**2. ContextMetricsService.captureBaseMetrics()** (packages/sidekick-daemon/src/context-metrics/context-metrics-service.ts)
Args: --session-id UUID -p /context
- Purpose: measure NORMAL system prompt overhead as baseline. Needs full context loaded.
- RISK 1: Same OAuth auth breakage
- RISK 2: --bare strips CLAUDE.md and system context — we'd measure a bare/minimal prompt instead of the real baseline. This defeats the entire purpose.
- Verdict: MUST NOT ADD --bare

### Hook Recursion Risk (The Real Problem --bare Would Have Solved)
spawnClaudeCli() passes NO env vars to spawned claude processes. So when Sidekick spawns claude -p for LLM calls, Claude Code hooks fire in that subprocess — including Sidekick's own hooks. Potential chain:
  LLM call → claude -p → Stop/UserPromptSubmit hooks → if reminder needed → another LLM call → ...
No recursion guard exists today.

### Recommendation: Ignore --bare; Fix Recursion Differently

**Adopt**: Nothing. --bare is not appropriate for either invocation site.

**Create follow-up bead**: Add SIDEKICK_SUBPROCESS=1 env var to spawnClaudeCli() options, pass it in the spawned process's env, and have Sidekick hooks short-circuit when this var is set. This gets the hook-recursion protection without breaking OAuth.

**Also note**: ContextMetricsService.captureBaseMetrics() is missing --no-session-persistence (unlike AnthropicCliProvider). Low-priority cleanup.
close_reason: Investigation complete. --bare is not appropriate for either invocation site (breaks OAuth). Hook recursion risk tracked in claude-code-sidekick-92qg.

title: E2E testing: Multi-agent, multi-worktree scenarios
description: Integration test scenarios: single subagent (hooks fire, reminders suppressed, verification tracked), concurrent subagents (state isolation, no clobbering), worktree subagent (cwd detection, path resolution, transcript stability), subagent verification passthrough (parent doesn't re-verify), UI rendering of subagent timeline and transcript drill-down.

title: State management: Agent-scoped vs session-scoped state isolation
description: Design and implement state isolation model. Session-scoped state (persona, summary, compaction history) stays shared. Agent-scoped state (verification tool tracking, reminder throttle) needs per-agent isolation. Concurrent subagents generate interleaved hook events — state keyed on session_id alone will race. Must key on (session_id, agent_id).

title: Reminders: Subagent-aware firing rules
description: Decide per-reminder whether it should fire in subagent context, be suppressed, or be adapted. Most reminders (pause-and-reflect, verify-completion, persona, stuck-loop) are parent-session concerns and should not fire when agent_id is present. Implement filtering based on agent_id presence in hook events.

title: UI: Add font size controls (+/-) to events and detail panes with persistent sizing
description: The font size in the events pane is too small. Add +/- controls at the top of the events and detail panes to increase or decrease font size. The selected size should be persisted locally (localStorage or cookie) so it survives page refreshes.

title: P&R: stage/unstage reminder triggers fire on PreToolUse even when tool execution is being blocked
description: When the PreToolUse threshold is hit and a blocking reminder is about to be dropped, the stage/unstage reminder triggers still fire during PreToolUse hook processing. This means reminders get staged or unstaged even though the tool execution itself is being blocked. These stage/unstage triggers should be moved to the PostToolUse hook flow so they only fire when the tool actually executes.
close_reason: Closed

title: UI: clicking tool call/result link scrolls to node but does not update detail panel
description: When the detail panel is open and the user clicks a tool 'call' or 'result' link, the events list scrolls to and selects the target node, but the detail panel content is not updated to reflect the newly selected node. Expected: detail panel should update to show the clicked node's details.
close_reason: Closed

title: Investigate statusline data absence in newsletter-digest session and tune prompts
description: Session 7f6e4399-688b-443a-bb37-44c13ffd9b03 (newsletter-digest) surfaced a problem: statusline wasn't showing data in the prototype. The session title changed when Scott questioned why data was missing. Investigate what changed in that session's statusline handling, identify root cause, and deliver prompt tuning + validation to fix it.
close_reason: Closed

title: promptSelect and promptInput in setup/prompts.ts also lack EOF handling
description: Same class of bug as odz: promptSelect and promptInput use rl.once('line') without a 'close' handler. If stdin closes without a newline, the promise never resolves. Fix: add rl.once('close') handler like promptConfirm now has. Also dev-mode.ts has its own duplicate promptConfirm that could be consolidated.
close_reason: Closed

title: Use distinct filenames for user vs project setup-status to prevent overwrites
description: Running claude from ~ caused the project-level auto-configure to overwrite ~/.sidekick/setup-status.json with a project-format payload, because both user and project scopes use the same filename. Use a different filename (e.g. user-setup-status.json vs setup-status.json) so they can never collide even when cwd is the home directory.
close_reason: Closed

title: Execute Playwright verification playbook for recent bug fixes
description: Follow the playbook at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-29-playwright-verification-playbook-design.md to verify 6 recent fixes (aox, fzk, u9c, 3pz, eup, pkj) using Playwright MCP browser tools in a fresh session. Run S1 (behavioral pattern matching) first to generate events, then verify S2-S6 in the UI.
close_reason: All 6 verification scenarios passed. S1(aox): command runner stripping works for direct, npx-prefixed, and negative cases. S3(u9c): analysis temporal ordering correct. S5(eup): no spurious unstaged events at session start. S4(3pz): hook detail panels show name, input, return value, duration, and expandable JSON. S2(fzk): no non-decision events in timeline, real decisions present. S6(pkj): decision titles are human-readable, subsystem and category in separate badges, consistent across all event types.

title: Inject brainstorming skill reminder to restrict questions to important ones
description: When the superpowers:brainstorming skill is invoked, Sidekick should inject a targeted reminder that guides the agent to restrict its clarifying questions to only the important, requirement-clarifying ones rather than asking exhaustive lists of questions that slow the user down. The brainstorming skill tends to generate long question lists that overwhelm — the reminder should encourage the agent to use its best judgment on implementation details and only ask about genuinely ambiguous requirements or architectural decisions.

title: Missing Decision node before re-staging persona reminder
description: At 16:41:18 the remember-your-persona reminder gets staged again, with a concurrent decision:recorded for 'Stage user-prompt reminder'. But the decision should (1) precede the staging action in the timeline, (2) have a descriptive title referencing the persona reminder not the user-prompt reminder, (3) show in the detail pane the decision factors: why was this reminder re-staged (e.g. prompt countdown expired, confidence below threshold, etc).
close_reason: Closed

title: Statusline event: relabel token count as 'chat tokens' and simplify bubble text
description: Statusline timeline events currently show 'Statusline: session summary' as bubble text. Issues: (1) 'session summary' is the displayMode, not useful to user — relabel bubble to just 'Statusline called' with duration, (2) token count in detail shows as generic 'tokens' — relabel as 'chat tokens' since it's the Claude Code conversation token count, (3) show hook script execution duration prominently, (4) show complete hook script inputs/outputs in detail pane.
close_reason: Closed

title: Hook event detail pane doesn't show complete raw input/output
description: HookDetail.tsx uses KeyValueRows which flattens large values. For hooks like SessionStart and UserPromptSubmit, the returnValue.additionalContext is a large string that gets truncated. Need: (1) expandable/collapsible sections for large values, (2) full raw JSON view option, (3) the hook:received should show the complete input payload including tool_input for PreToolUse hooks.
close_reason: Closed

title: LLM operation start events missing 'Start' in bubble label
description: Timeline bubbles for snarky-message:start, resume-message:start, and session-summary:start show only the operation name (e.g. 'Snarky Message') without 'Start'. The finish events show content (e.g. 'Snarky Message: <text>'). All bookend events should consistently show Start/Finish in the bubble text. Fix in generateLabel() in timeline-api.ts — append ' Start' to all :start event labels.
close_reason: Closed

title: Session Analysis emits mismatched start/finish events
description: The session analysis lifecycle emits 2 session-summary:start and 2 session-summary:finish events but they don't match 1:1. First pair: start at :41, finish at :46 with 'New Session'. Second: start at :45 (duplicate?), finish at 16:41:00 with real title. The async snarky/resume LLM calls complete after session-summary:finish, which is correct if they're independent async tasks. But the start/finish pairing is broken — need to ensure each start has exactly one matching finish.
close_reason: Closed

title: Consumed reminder detail pane missing rendered reminder text
description: When clicking a 'consumed: reminder-type' event in the timeline, the detail pane shows only the reminder ID and action type. It should show the actual rendered reminder text that was injected. The raw logs don't capture the text — need to log the rendered content at consumption time so the UI can display it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Decision label 'BulkProcessingComplete' misleading for fresh sessions
description: On a fresh /clear session, the first UserPromptSubmit triggers a Decision event with reason 'BulkProcessingComplete - analyzing full transcript'. This label is misleading because no bulk processing started (it's a new session). The decision should describe the actual evaluation factors: confidence checks, prompt countdowns, trigger conditions.
close_reason: Closed

title: Duplicate reminder staging on SessionStart+UserPromptSubmit overlap
description: When a /clear triggers both SessionStart and UserPromptSubmit hooks concurrently, the daemon stages remember-your-persona 3 times and user-profile 2 times. Each hook handler independently stages the same reminders without checking if they're already staged. Need deduplication logic in the reminder staging pipeline.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add VC skill reminder to create beads for skipped/discovered work
description: The verification-before-completion skill should remind agents to create new beads for any work that was skipped, deferred, or identified as pre-existing issues during the current task. This prevents discovered work from silently falling through the cracks.

title: ReminderOrchestrator logs reminder:unstaged events without sessionId context
description: Same class of bug as h92 (fixed in PR #100). The ReminderOrchestrator (feature-reminders/src/orchestrator.ts:82-90, 130-139) calls logEvent(this.deps.logger, ReminderEvents.reminderUnstaged(...)) where this.deps.logger is the daemon root logger (initialized at daemon.ts:338 with this.logger). This logger has no context.sessionId binding, so reminder:unstaged events from cascade operations (P&R→VC unstage, VC consumed→P&R unstage) are invisible in the UI timeline. Fix: create a child logger with sessionId context before logging, similar to the staging-service.ts fix.
close_reason: Merged in PR #102. Fixed 3 logEvent call sites using root logger without sessionId context (orchestrator.ts x2, daemon.ts x1). Audit confirmed no other gaps.

title: Investigate missing reminder:staged events in UI
description: User reports seeing unstaged and consumed reminder bubbles in the transcript UI, but no staged events. Need to determine if this is a daemon logging issue (not emitting reminder:staged events) or a UI filtering/display bug. The UI filter mapping and classifyLineCategory look correct for reminder:staged.
close_reason: Root cause: StagingServiceCore used daemon root logger (empty context) for reminder:staged events. Fix: child logger with sessionId bound. PR #100.

title: Add BulkProcessingComplete lifecycle events (starting/complete)
description: BulkProcessingComplete needs a full lifecycle with starting and complete events, similar to session-summary:start/finish pattern. Currently only has a single event. Requires changes in sidekick-core event emission and UI timeline/transcript rendering.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #112 — one-shot guard prevents spurious BulkProcessingComplete after /clear, lifecycle events added

title: Detail panel State tab — implement state snapshot viewer
description: The Detail panel's State tab is not implemented. When clicking a transcript line and switching to the State tab, it should show relevant Sidekick state snapshots (session summary, persona, transcript metrics, etc.) at that point in time. Currently renders an empty StateTab component.
close_reason: Closed

title: Investigate missing reminder:staged events on session start
description: Some reminders get staged on session start but their events don't appear in the timeline or transcript. May be a sidekick-core logging gap — events might be emitted before the log file is ready, or the session context may not be set when early reminders are staged. Needs investigation in sidekick-core event emission path.
close_reason: Already fixed by PR #100 (ca3c74f). Root cause: StagingServiceCore.stageReminder() used daemon root logger (no context.sessionId). Fix: create child logger with sessionId before logEvent(). LogEvents.reminderStaged() is only emitted from staging-service.ts:149 — the single emission point is patched and tested.

Implement D25 (scroll) from docs/plans/2026-03-14-ui-ux-coherence-design.md. Synchronize scrolling by timestamp.

SCOPE:
- Create useScrollSync.ts hook — scroll coordinator that maps timestamps to vertical positions
- Each panel registers with coordinator: panelId, ref, timestamps[]
- On scroll in any panel: map scroll position → timestamp → corresponding position in other panels
- Spacer insertion: when one panel has events in a time window and another doesn't, insert invisible spacer divs to maintain horizontal temporal alignment
- Algorithm: build unified timestamp grid across all visible panels, compute spacer heights, insert spacer elements
- Performance: only recompute spacers when panel content changes, not on every scroll

FILES: src/hooks/useScrollSync.ts (CREATE), src/components/transcript/Transcript.tsx, src/components/transcript/SubagentTranscript.tsx

DEPENDS ON: Task 12 (needs multi-panel layout)

APPROACH: TDD for timestamp grid computation and spacer logic. Playwright functional testing for scroll behavior. End with all tests passing, reviewed and clean code.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Scrolling one panel scrolls the other to matching timestamp
- Spacers inserted to maintain horizontal alignment
- Time codes align horizontally across panels
- Smooth scrolling, no jitter
- Performance acceptable with large transcripts
- Build and typecheck pass, all tests pass

title: Investigate worktree agent completion verification gap
description:
When a main agent dispatches work to subagents in isolated git worktrees, the main agent can claim 'done' before the worktree changes are properly integrated. The verification hooks fire on the main agent's workspace, which doesn't contain the subagent's modifications — so build/typecheck/lint/test checks pass vacuously.

Patterns to investigate:
1. Main agent cherry-picks/merges worktree commits back to its own branch, then verification hooks catch real state
2. Subagents push their worktree branches and/or create PRs directly, main agent just orchestrates
3. Verification hooks become worktree-aware — detect pending worktree branches with unmerged commits

The PR #69 session exposed this: the stop hook fired verification warnings because the main workspace had no changes, even though the subagent had already built/tested/pushed successfully from its worktree.
notes:
## Experiment Complete (2026-04-05)

claude-hook-probe test harness executed successfully — 25 questions answered across 13 phases, 429 hook events captured.

### Key findings documented in:
- **docs/CLAUDE-CODE-SUBAGENT-HOOK-BEHAVIOR.md** — Standalone reference with all empirical facts and Sidekick architectural implications
- **~/src/projects/claude-hook-probe/REPORT.md** — Raw test report with full evidence

### Architectural answers for this issue:
- Worktree subagent cwd = worktree path, detectable via cwd != CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR in hooks
- Transcript stays at project root during worktree sessions (no file migration)
- SubagentStart/SubagentStop are reliable (13/13), contrary to bug reports
- Subagent control requires two-phase pattern: SubagentStart contract + PreToolUse trigger
- State must be keyed on (session_id, agent_id) due to concurrent hook interleaving
- CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is hook-only (absent from all Bash tool contexts)
- stop_hook_active field on SubagentStop needs future handling (tracked as claude-hook-probe-wgz P3)
close_reason: All 25 empirical questions answered. Findings documented in docs/CLAUDE-CODE-SUBAGENT-HOOK-BEHAVIOR.md. Architecture decisions for worktree/subagent handling are now evidence-based.

title: Persona voice favors user interests over character universe

The persona prompt/injection should emphasize that the character stays in their own universe for metaphors, references, and humor — the user profile is for understanding the audience, not for sourcing the character's vocabulary. title: Spec: New feature integration — LLM calls, task engine, context window in spec
description:
Produce Section 6 of IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md: New feature integration.

## Context
PHASE2-AUDIT §3 identified features that exist in the backend but have no UI representation, plus 3 entirely new features recommended for addition. These need to be integrated into the implementation spec with concrete component placement and data source definitions.

## Input Documents
- PHASE2-AUDIT §3.1 (readiness assessment for all 19 requirements — scores and backend status)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §3.2 (3 new features: F-10 LLM Call Timeline, G-11 Completion Classifier Detail, Context Window Utilization)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §3.3 (tier priority recommendations: Tier 1/2/3)
- REQUIREMENTS.md §5 (G-1 through G-10 feature descriptions)
- DO NOT reference MONITORING-UI.md (deprecated)

## Scope
For each Tier 1 and Tier 2 feature:
1. Data source: which file(s) or log event(s) provide the data
2. Component placement: where in the four-panel layout (session selector, timeline, transcript, detail panel)
3. Interaction pattern: how user discovers and drills into the feature
4. Dependencies: what backend instrumentation exists vs what's missing (use readiness scores from §3.1)

Tier 3 features: document as future work only, no detailed spec needed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tier 1 features (F-10, G-2 enhancement, G-11, F-7) fully specified with data source, component, interaction
- Tier 2 features (G-7 enhancement, G-8 enhancement, F-1 enhancement, G-5 enhancement) specified at design level
- Tier 3 features listed as future work with one-line scope
- Any missing backend instrumentation flagged as prerequisite tasks
close_reason: Closed

title: Spec: Performance requirements — event count targets, rendering budget, virtual scrolling
description:
Produce Section 5 of IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md: Performance requirements.

## Context
This task is largely GREEN-FIELD — neither REQUIREMENTS.md nor PHASE2-AUDIT.md define concrete performance targets. The existing docs tell us WHAT to worry about (large sessions, log file sizes, live mode polling) but not the numbers. This task authors new requirements based on observed data volumes and UX expectations.

## Known Constraints (from existing docs)
- Log files: NDJSON, max 10MB per file, 5 rotated files (PHASE2-AUDIT §2.4)
- Large sessions can produce 1000+ events — known to lag without virtual scrolling (sidekick-n4lx epic)
- Live mode needs real-time updates (REQUIREMENTS.md F-9) — polling interval TBD
- File-based data source, no WebSocket (REQUIREMENTS.md §6-7)
- DO NOT reference MONITORING-UI.md (deprecated)

## Scope
Define concrete targets for:
1. Max event count before virtual scrolling activates (propose a threshold)
2. Polling interval for live mode (balance responsiveness vs CPU/IO)
3. Rendering budget per frame (target 60fps, how many DOM updates per cycle)
4. Log file ingestion: max file size to parse in-browser, chunking strategy for large logs
5. Initial load time target for a typical session (e.g., 500 events)
6. Memory budget for in-browser state timeline

## Acceptance Criteria
- Concrete numeric targets for each of the 6 areas above
- Rationale for each target (based on observed data volumes or UX research)
- Identified risks if targets are exceeded, with fallback strategies
- Virtual scrolling library recommendation (or build-vs-buy decision)
close_reason: Section 5 written with all 6 performance targets, reviewed and cross-ref errors fixed. PR #65.

title: Spec: Component-to-type wiring — map React components to @sidekick/types
description:
Produce Section 4 of IMPLEMENTATION-SPEC.md: Component-to-type wiring.

## Context
The v2 prototype (PR #28, feat(ui): v2 four-panel prototype with LED gutter) has 21 React components running on mock data. The PHASE2-AUDIT inventoried the OLD UI's 16 event types (§1.2) and found gaps (§1.3-1.4), but the v2 prototype's components have NOT been inventoried against @sidekick/types. This task must do that fresh inventory.

## Input Documents
- packages/sidekick-ui/src/components/ (the actual v2 prototype — 21 .tsx files)
- packages/sidekick-ui/src/types.ts (current UI type definitions)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §1.1 (canonical @sidekick/types inventory)
- PHASE2-AUDIT §1.3-1.4 (gaps and drift analysis — based on old UI, verify against v2)
- REQUIREMENTS.md §4-5 (feature → view mapping)
- DO NOT reference MONITORING-UI.md (deprecated)

## Scope
1. Inventory each of the 21 v2 prototype components: current props, mock data shape, intended feature
2. Map each component to its @sidekick/types source types
3. Identify props that need new types or modified interfaces
4. Define data transformation functions needed (backend response → component props)
5. Flag components that exist in the prototype but have no corresponding backend data source yet

## Acceptance Criteria
- Table mapping all 21 components to @sidekick/types with import paths
- Each component's props interface defined or referenced
- Transformation functions identified where backend shape ≠ component props shape
- Gap list: components needing types that don't exist yet in @sidekick/types

title: UI Implementation: wire prototype to real data, add tests, performance optimization
description:
Epic: wire the Sidekick Monitoring UI prototype to real data, add tests, optimize performance.

PLANNING DIRECTIVE: Plan directionally for the long-term, concretely for the short term, and revisit the plan after each iteration.

APPROACH: Backend-first (CLI/daemon emitting correctly) -> tracer bullets (end-to-end slices) -> layer-by-layer gap closure -> polish.

PHASES:
- Phase 1 (COMPLETE): Event system gaps — wire missing logEvent() calls, add reminder:not-staged event type, enrich reminder payloads with forensic context.
- Phase 2 (NEXT): Tracer bullets — identify and implement 2-3 end-to-end steel threads through all layers (daemon events -> NDJSON logs -> Vite backend -> SSE -> React UI) to prove the architecture before investing in breadth.
- Phase 3: Layer-by-layer gap closure — fill remaining gaps per layer after architecture is proven.
- Phase 4: Performance optimization (virtual scrolling, incremental updates), integration tests with real log fixtures.

DESIGN DOCS:
- docs/plans/2026-03-13-ui-implementation-epic-design.md (master epic decomposition)
- docs/plans/2026-03-13-reminder-event-enrichment-design.md (reminder:not-staged + payload enrichment design)

- docs/plans/2026-03-14-reminder-payload-enrichment.md (implementation plan, executed)
