title: Add tests for TranscriptEntrySchema and TranscriptUuidSchema
description:
Task 9.3.10 added two new Zod schemas to transcript-schemas.ts but no corresponding tests were added:

1. TranscriptEntrySchema - minimal schema using z.object({}).passthrough() for raw transcript JSONL entries
2. TranscriptUuidSchema - schema for extracting UUID from transcript entries with z.object({ uuid: z.string().optional() }).passthrough()

These schemas are used in transcript-service.ts:
- TranscriptEntrySchema: getTranscript() line 378, processTranscriptFile() line 1037
- TranscriptUuidSchema: parseUuid() line 654

The existing test file packages/sidekick-core/src/state/__tests__/transcript-schemas.test.ts tests PersistedTranscriptStateSchema and CompactionHistorySchema but not these new schemas.

Files:
- packages/sidekick-core/src/state/transcript-schemas.ts (schema definitions)
- packages/sidekick-core/src/state/__tests__/transcript-schemas.test.ts (test file to update)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tests verify TranscriptEntrySchema accepts valid objects and rejects non-objects (null, arrays, primitives)
2. Tests verify TranscriptEntrySchema.passthrough() allows unknown fields
3. Tests verify TranscriptUuidSchema extracts uuid field correctly
4. Tests verify TranscriptUuidSchema returns undefined for missing uuid
5. Tests verify TranscriptUuidSchema rejects non-string uuid values
6. All tests pass

title: staging-service listReminders/listConsumedReminders bypass StateService
description:
In staging-service.ts, the listReminders() and listConsumedReminders() methods use direct readFile() calls instead of stateService.read(), violating the Phase 9.3 acceptance criteria 'No direct fs read/write for state files outside StateService'.

Context:
- stageReminder() correctly uses stateService.write() (line 130)
- readReminder() correctly uses stateService.read() (line 164)
- But listReminders() uses readFile() directly (line 204)
- And listConsumedReminders() uses readFile() directly (line 288)

This was discovered during code review of task 9.3.10 which added Zod validation. The validation was added correctly using StagedReminderSchema.safeParse(), but the underlying file access pattern is inconsistent.

Files:
- packages/sidekick-core/src/staging-service.ts

Root cause: These methods iterate over multiple files in a directory and read each one. The original implementation predates StateService and was never migrated.
acceptance_criteria:
1. listReminders() uses stateService.read() with StagedReminderSchema instead of direct readFile()
2. listConsumedReminders() uses stateService.read() with StagedReminderSchema instead of direct readFile()
3. No direct fs.readFile calls remain in staging-service.ts for reading reminder JSON files
4. Existing tests pass
5. Build and typecheck pass

title: Fix uninstall dry-run path label for .sidekick/.gitignore format
description: When the new .sidekick/.gitignore format is present, uninstall's dry-run action records path: path.join(projectDir, '.gitignore') (the root file). This tells the user it would remove root .gitignore when it would actually remove .sidekick/.gitignore. The summary detection (collectDetectionSummary) already does correct format-aware detection — the dry-run stub at ~line 223-229 in uninstall.ts does not. Fix: detect which format is present before constructing the dry-run action, and use the correct path in the action record. Discovered-from: feat/sidekick-gitignore-subdirectory PR review.

title: Remove isValidPathSegment duplicate in sidekick-ui/server/utils.ts
description: packages/sidekick-ui/server/utils.ts:16-22 contains a byte-for-byte duplicate of isValidPathSegment from packages/sidekick-core/src/staging-paths.ts:73. The correct pattern (used by session-log-writer.ts and cli-staging-reader.ts) is to import from @sidekick/core. Replace the inline copy in utils.ts with an import. Discovered during simplify pass on fix/session-log-writer-post-ship-bugs (not introduced by that branch).

title: Replace setTimeout-based async write verification in session-log-writer tests
description: session-log-writer.test.ts uses await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100)) to wait for fire-and-forget writes to complete. Classic timing-based test antipattern — flaky on slow CI, unnecessarily slow on fast machines. Replace with condition-based polling: read the file in a retry loop until content appears or a timeout elapses. File: packages/sidekick-core/src/__tests__/session-log-writer.test.ts. Found by RALF plan review (e9d4).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #176 (fix/session-log-writer-post-ship-bugs)

title: Update design docs for subagent-aware hook pipeline (flow.md, CLI.md, CORE-RUNTIME.md, DAEMON.md, SCHEMA-CONTRACTS.md)
description: Once sidekick-z63.5 lands, the design docs that enumerate hook events need updating to reflect SubagentStart and SubagentStop as first-class hook types. Specific touchpoints: docs/design/flow.md §3.2 (Event Schema) and §5 (Complete Hook Flows) currently enumerate only the 7 existing hook events; docs/design/CLI.md §3.1.1 (Hook Input Structure) should list the new payload fields including agent_id, agent_type, agent_transcript_path, last_assistant_message; docs/design/CORE-RUNTIME.md §3.5 (Handler Registration) should note that new hook names are registered generically via HOOK_NAMES; docs/design/SCHEMA-CONTRACTS.md and docs/design/DAEMON.md also enumerate hook events and should be reviewed for consistency. Can land as part of z63.5's completion gate or as a follow-up PR. Discovered while planning sidekick-z63.5.

title: Update CLAUDE-CODE-SUBAGENT-HOOK-BEHAVIOR.md with full official-docs field lists
description: Probe doc (docs/CLAUDE-CODE-SUBAGENT-HOOK-BEHAVIOR.md) is silent on several fields the official Claude Code docs (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) now enumerate for SubagentStart/SubagentStop: last_assistant_message, explicit confirmation of agent_id/agent_type on SubagentStart (probe implied these were absent), permission_mode presence on SubagentStop but absence on SubagentStart. The probe's claim that stop_hook_active was 'never populated in testing' also directly contradicts the docs example payload showing stop_hook_active: false; this needs empirical re-verification against current Claude Code and the probe doc updated accordingly. Discovered while planning sidekick-z63.5.

title: SubagentStart context injection
description: Implement SubagentStart hook handler to inject context into subagents at launch time. Probe proved additionalContext injects as <system-reminder> before task instructions. Design what Sidekick should inject: project context, behavioral contracts for verification reporting, lightweight persona/session awareness. Uses hookSpecificOutput wrapper.

title: Statusline: Subagent activity indicators
description: Show subagent activity in the statusline — active subagent count/indicator, ensure token/cost metrics include subagent usage, show worktree branch when applicable.

title: Session summary: Reflect subagent activity
description: Session summary should reflect delegated subagent work without being confused by interleaved tool calls. Subagents don't need their own summaries, but the parent summary should capture the full picture of what was accomplished across agents.

title: Migrate persona-transition.ts to use SessionStateAccessor
description: persona-transition.ts writes snarky-message.json and deletes resume-message.json directly through stateService.write()/delete(), bypassing SessionStateAccessor and the state journal. The transient gap is tolerable (next summary cycle re-writes via accessor), but migrating would close it. Discovered during state snapshot viewer implementation (d9b).

title: Migrate transcript-metrics and llm-metrics writers to SessionStateAccessor
description: transcript-persistence.ts and InstrumentedLLMProvider.persist() write state files directly through stateService.write(), bypassing SessionStateAccessor. This means their state changes are not captured in the state journal. Migrating them would enable transcriptMetrics and llmMetrics fields in the State tab. Discovered during state snapshot viewer implementation (d9b).

title: transcript-api tests: cover agent_progress/pr-link drop in subagent path and listSubagents .json edge case
description:
Minor findings from code review of PR #173 (test/transcript-api-coverage):
1. listSubagents does not test that agent-*.json (without 'l') files are excluded — filter has two conditions but only one is exercised
2. parseSubagentTranscript silently drops pr-link and agent_progress types (no onExtra callback) — undocumented, no test asserting this behavior
3. agent_progress backward-scan test covers 'skips non-tool-use types' but not 'attaches to most-recent tool-use not an earlier one' — would need two tool-use entries before agent_progress
These are test quality improvements, not production bugs.

title: dev-mode: add clean-all-projects command using ~/.sidekick/projects/ data

title: Reconcile stale used/scopes metadata in user-status API key entries
description: During user-status reconciliation, only the top-level 'status' field is compared. Object entries with matching status but different 'used' or 'scopes' values (e.g. key moved from env to user scope) are not updated. Consider deep-comparing the full shape so metadata stays accurate when keys move between scopes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Migrate legacy string-format API key entries to object format during doctor reconciliation
description: During user-status reconciliation, legacy string entries like 'pending-validation' are normalized via toScopeStatus which maps them to 'missing'. This means a stale 'pending-validation' entry won't be upgraded to the new object format when the detected status is also 'missing'. Should treat any non-object entry (except 'not-required') as needing migration to the new {used, status, scopes} format regardless of effective status match.
close_reason: Closed

title: Doctor: downgrade plugin+dev-mode coexistence from warning to info
description: When both the sidekick plugin and dev-mode are detected, doctor reports a warning icon (⚠) and the overall status becomes 'needs attention'. This is not actually a problem — both can coexist fine. Doctor should still report the coexistence, but as info-level (• or ℹ) so it doesn't trigger the overall unhealthy status or suggest --fix.

title: Remove LEGACY_USER_STATUS_FILENAME migration code

Since Scott is the only user, the migration has already run. Remove: LEGACY_USER_STATUS_FILENAME constant, legacyUserStatusPath getter, migrateFromLegacyUserStatus() method, legacy fs.access check in detectUserScope(), and all associated tests. Simplify the ENOENT handler in getUserStatus() back to returning null.

title: Create skill to digest repeated corrections into reminder configuration
description: When the user repeatedly gives the same feedback ('did you do X?', 'don't forget to Y'), this signals a missing reminder. Create a sidekick skill that analyzes patterns of repeated user corrections (from session history, self-improving-agent invocations, feedback memories) and proposes new reminder injection rules. The skill should: (1) identify recurring correction patterns, (2) draft reminder text and injection conditions, (3) present the proposed reminder for user approval, (4) persist the new reminder configuration. This is use-case-specific — each correction pattern maps to a specific reminder with appropriate trigger conditions. Related to the existing self-improving-agent skill but focused specifically on the reminder pipeline rather than general behavioral rules.

title: Extract shared Collapsible component from detail panel views

These are not real errors — build and tests pass in both trees. Likely the LSP is picking up worktree files without their node_modules context. Investigate whether tsconfig or LSP config can exclude worktree paths.

title: Experiment with /btw command for session titling

Particularly interesting use case: automatically titling a session after pivot detection occurs (e.g., when the conversation shifts focus). Investigate how /btw works, what triggers make sense, and whether this can integrate with Sidekick's existing pivot detection or session tracking.

title: fix(context-metrics): address PR #21 review feedback
description:
PR #21 Copilot review left 4 inline comments. After investigation, 3 are valid:

## 1. Remove unnecessary INTEGRATION_TESTS gate (line 211)
The `describe.skipIf(!process.env.INTEGRATION_TESTS)` block at test line 211 wraps fully-mocked tests (spawnClaudeCli is vi.mock'd). These never hit the real CLI, so they should run as normal unit tests. Remove the `skipIf`.

## 2. Replace real sleep with deterministic wait (line 222)
`initializeWithCapture()` uses `setTimeout(r, 500)` to wait for async capture. This is flaky on slow CI. Replace with polling for expected logger state or use vi.useFakeTimers + flush.

## 3. Add sessionId to warn log metadata (service lines 259, 270)
Two of the three warn paths in `captureBaseMetrics()` are missing `sessionId` in their meta objects:
- Line 259: "CLI stdout does not appear to be /context output" — has stdoutLength/stdoutPreview but no sessionId
- Line 270: "Failed to parse /context table from CLI stdout" — same issue
- Line 250: "CLI stdout was empty..." — already has sessionId (correct)

## Rejected: Comment about message mismatch (Copilot line 304)
Copilot claimed test asserts 'CLI stdout was empty' but production logs 'CLI stdout was empty — /context produced no output'. This is WRONG — the test at line 290 correctly asserts the full message. Copilot cited the wrong line number.

### Files
- `packages/sidekick-daemon/src/context-metrics/context-metrics-service.ts` (lines 259, 270)
- `packages/sidekick-daemon/src/context-metrics/__tests__/context-metrics-cli-capture.test.ts` (lines 211, 222)
close_reason: Merged via PR #22

title: Watch user profile file and stage user profile reminder on change
description: Monitor the user profile file for changes during a session. When a change is detected (file modification), stage the user profile reminder for injection so the agent picks up updated user context (interests, preferences, name) without requiring a new session. This ensures profile updates are reflected in the active conversation.

title: Stage persona reminder via post-compact hook
description: After context compaction, the persona voice may be lost. Add a post-compact hook that stages the persona reminder injection, ensuring the active persona is re-injected into the conversation after compaction occurs. This preserves persona continuity across compaction boundaries.

title: Research and enrich persona files with authentic character quotes and profiles
description:
Use web search (Gemini or Claude web search) to research famous quotes and character profiles for all 27 character personas, then update the persona YAML files with more authentic, source-accurate content.

## Scope

For each persona in assets/sidekick/personas/*.yaml (excluding disabled.yaml and sidekick.yaml):

1. **Character Profile Research**: Verify and enrich the theme, personality_traits, and tone_traits against canonical character descriptions
2. **Famous Quotes**: Find actual iconic quotes from the source material (TV shows, movies, books, games) 
3. **Update statusline_empty_messages**: Replace generic/improvised messages with authentic quotes adapted for a coding context (the key value prop — real quotes twisted to fit software engineering)
4. **Update snarky_examples**: Ensure these feel true to the character voice
5. **Update snarky_welcome_examples**: Use authentic return/greeting patterns from the character

## Personas (27 total)

bones, c3po, captain-kirk, cavil, darth-vader, dilbert, eddie, emh, emperor-palpatine, george, glados, hudson, jarvis, kramer, marvin, mr-spock, mr-t, pointy-haired-boss, ripley, rodney-mckay, scotty, seven-of-nine, sheldon, skippy, tars, yoda, avasarala

## Approach

- Use parallel subagents with web search to research 3-4 personas each
- Prioritize personas with fewer/weaker quotes (smaller files like kramer, hudson, ripley, mr-t)
- Keep the YAML structure identical — only update content fields
- Aim for 15-20 statusline_empty_messages per persona (some have as few as 13, best have 18-20)
- Preserve any existing messages that are already authentic quotes
- New quotes should be adapted to coding/programming context while remaining recognizable

## Acceptance Criteria

- Build passes. Typecheck passes.
- All persona YAML files parse correctly
- Each persona has at least 15 statusline_empty_messages
- Quotes are recognizably from the source character
- Existing good quotes preserved, weak ones replaced
close_reason: All 27 persona files enriched with authentic source-material quotes and vivid character profiles. Quotes: 18-20 statusline, 5-7 snarky, 4-5 welcome per persona. Themes: 200-400ch with vivid descriptions. Traits: 6-8 personality, 5-6 tone. All validated, build passes, tests pass.

title: Context injector: Magic question filter ('Do you have any questions for me?')
description:
Implement a two-pass question filtering technique as a context injector:

**The pattern**:
1. After the agent reviews a task/spec, ask: 'Do you have any questions for me?'
2. Scan the returned questions. If they can all be answered by reading more code, respond: 'Read more code to answer your own questions.'
3. If the agent comes back again, the remaining questions are the *real* ones — genuine ambiguities that require human input.

**Rationale**: Agents often ask clarifying questions that are actually answerable from the codebase. This filter forces the agent to exhaust code-reading before escalating to the user, reducing unnecessary interruptions while surfacing truly important decisions.

**Implementation ideas**:
- Could be a skill invoked during planning/brainstorming phases
- Question classifier: 'Can this be answered by reading code?' (heuristic or LLM-based)
- Integration with the brainstorming skill's question-gathering phase
- Could track question resolution patterns over time to improve the filter
close_reason: Not needed - decided against implementing this feature

title: Context injector: Post-build reflection prompts
description:
After completing a feature or significant implementation, inject reflection prompts into the agent's context:

1. 'Now that you built it, what would you have done differently?'
2. 'What can we refactor?'

**Rationale**: The agent discovers pain points during building — just like a human developer. That context (awkward APIs, repeated patterns, missed abstractions) is most valuable immediately after building, before it disappears with session compaction. Capture it as actionable refactoring suggestions or new beads.

**Implementation ideas**:
- Hook into task/bead completion events
- Inject as a post-completion prompt in the session
- Could generate refactoring beads automatically from the agent's answers
- May tie into the code-review/code-simplifier pipeline as an additional reflection step

title: Fix ENOTEMPTY race in sidekick-ui missing-files.test.ts cleanup
description: packages/sidekick-ui/server/__tests__/missing-files.test.ts afterEach cleanup calls rmSync(testRoot, { recursive: true, force: true }) but intermittently fails with ENOTEMPTY. This is a race condition where coverage instrumentation or parallel tests leave files in the temp directory between the existsSync check and the rmSync call. Surfaces only under pnpm test:coverage (V8 instrumentation slows execution, widening the race window). Fix: use retry logic or fs.rm with maxRetries option.

title: Add explicit chmod 0o600 on setup-status.json writes
description: setup-status-service.ts relies on OS umask for file permissions on setup-status.json. Add explicit chmod 0o600 after writes to harden file permissions at both user and project scope. Location: packages/sidekick-core/src/setup-status-service.ts
close_reason: Closed

close_reason: Closed

title: Change default logging format from pretty to json
description: core.defaults.yaml sets logging.format: pretty but the structured logging system is designed for JSON output. Change default to json so logs are machine-parseable out of the box. Location: assets/sidekick/defaults/core.defaults.yaml
close_reason: Closed

title: Custom spinner verbs per personality for Claude Code

Currently Claude Code shows generic verbs like 'Thinking...', 'Working...' etc. These should be personality-aware — e.g. Mr. T says 'Getting results...', a pirate says 'Charting course...'. Requires: (1) defining verb lists in each persona's YAML/profile, (2) a mechanism to inject them into Claude Code's spinner config or hook output, (3) session-aware so it changes with persona switches.

The goal is to eliminate the npx prefix for day-to-day usage. Update documentation after implementation.

title: Inject persona-reset reminder when persona is disabled mid-session

Without this, Claude continues in the previous persona's voice until the session ends or context compacts. Edge case — not needed for MVP of persona injection (sidekick-n35d).

title: Add 'install' command alias for setup
description:
Add an 'install' command alias so that 'pnpm sidekick install' is equivalent to 'pnpm sidekick setup'. This is a convenience alias — users may intuitively reach for 'install' instead of 'setup'.

Acceptance criteria:
- 'pnpm sidekick install' runs the same code path as 'pnpm sidekick setup'
- All setup flags/options work with install
- Help text mentions the alias
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Triple verify-completion staging in transcript batch causes ENOTEMPTY error
description: During a large transcript catch-up (181 lines processed at once), the reminders:stage-stop-reminders handler fires multiple times in the same batch, each staging verify-completion.json to the same path. In session 47ac47ad, it staged 3 times at timestamp 1770507221811, then hit ENOTEMPTY (errno -66) when trying to rmdir the stage/Stop/ directory. The handler needs to deduplicate staging within a single transcript processing batch.

title: Investigate why setup wizard didn't run in agents-config project
description:
User ran sidekick in ~/src/projects/agents-config/ via --plugin-dir but the project has no .sidekick/setup-status.json file, only auto-created logs/ and sessions/ directories.

**Questions to answer**:
1. Did the setup wizard ever actually get invoked in that project?
2. If auto-configure is enabled (user preference shows autoConfigureProjects: true), why didn't it auto-configure?
3. Is the plugin startup correctly triggering setup for new projects?

**Investigation steps**:
- Check how plugin startup detects unconfigured projects
- Check if auto-configure code path is being executed
- Review plugin logs if any
- Test the flow: start claude with --plugin-dir in a fresh project

**Acceptance criteria**:
- Root cause identified
- Fix created if bug found, or explanation if working-as-designed
notes: FINDING: shouldAutoConfigureProject() is defined but only called in tests, never in production code. Auto-configure is a half-implemented feature - user preference is stored but never acted upon during plugin startup. title: Use factories for typed object defaults instead of inline placeholders
description:
Currently we use inline object placeholders like `{ turnCount: 0, toolsThisTurn: 0 }` which are error-prone when types evolve (see sidekick-b7l where toolCount was missing).

Consider:
1. Factory functions that always return complete typed objects: `createDefaultStagingMetrics()`
2. Zod schemas with .default() for automatic defaults
3. Builder patterns for complex objects

Benefits:
- Type safety: factories return the full type, can't forget fields
- Single source of truth: defaults defined once, not scattered
- Easier refactoring: add a field in one place

Example:
```typescript
// Instead of:
const metrics = reminder.stagedAt ?? { turnCount: 0, toolsThisTurn: 0, toolCount: 0 }

// Use:
const metrics = reminder.stagedAt ?? createDefaultStagingMetrics()
```

title: Daemon startup race condition causes token-not-found warnings
description:
Hooks firing before daemon fully starts trigger 'Daemon token not found' warnings. System recovers via graceful degradation but logs are noisy (warning on every hook invocation).

## Root Cause

`ensureDaemonForHook()` in hook-command.ts:463 returns a boolean indicating whether the daemon started, but the return value is **ignored**. Hooks always proceed to create IpcService and call send(), which tries to read the token file — fails with ENOENT — and logs a warning per invocation.

## Solution: Daemon Health State File

### 1. Thread daemonAvailable through to handleHookCommand
- Capture return value of `ensureDaemonForHook()`
- Pass it via HandleHookOptions to `handleHookCommand()`
- Skip IPC send when daemon unavailable (CLI-side handlers still run)

### 2. New daemon health state file: .sidekick/state/daemon-health.json
- Schema: `{ status: 'unknown' | 'healthy' | 'failed', lastCheckedAt: string, error?: string }`
- Written by CLI in `ensureDaemonForHook()` on state transitions only
- Transitions: unknown→healthy (INFO), unknown→failed (ERROR), failed→healthy (INFO), healthy→failed (ERROR)
- Log-once-per-transition eliminates noisy repeated warnings

### 3. Daemon self-reports healthy on successful start
- daemon.ts:332 (after 'Daemon started successfully') writes healthy status
- Clears any previous failed state from prior runs

### 4. Statusline integration
- Statusline reads daemon-health.json as part of health check
- If setup is healthy but daemon is failed → distinct degraded statusline message
- Lower priority than setup issues (setup problems are more fundamental)

### 5. Upgrade log level
- Daemon start failure: ERROR (not warn) — without daemon, sidekick is effectively inert
acceptance_criteria: Startup race condition eliminated or warnings suppressed during expected startup window. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes:
Acceptance Criteria:
- ensureDaemonForHook return value threaded through to handleHookCommand
- IPC send skipped when daemon unavailable (CLI handlers still fire)
- daemon-health.json state file with Zod schema in @sidekick/types
- State transitions logged once (ERROR for failures, INFO for recovery)
- Daemon writes healthy on successful start
- Statusline surfaces daemon-failed as degraded state
- No more repeated 'token not found' warnings per hook invocation
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: CLIStagingReader duplicates validation pattern without architectural comment
description:
The CLIStagingReader in feature-reminders duplicates the Zod validation pattern from StagingServiceCore but without documentation explaining why.

Context:
- StagingServiceCore.listReminders() logs validation failures via this.options.logger
- CLIStagingReader.listReminders() silently skips invalid files (no logger available in CLI context)
- Both use StagedReminderSchema.safeParse() correctly

The duplication exists because:
1. CLI process cannot access DaemonContext.staging (runs in separate process)
2. CLI has no logger instance available for warning messages
3. CLIStagingReader is a lightweight read-only interface for CLI consumption

This architectural decision should be documented in the file header or inline comments so future maintainers understand why the pattern differs.

Files:
- packages/feature-reminders/src/cli-staging-reader.ts

Related:
- packages/sidekick-core/src/staging-service.ts (the daemon-side equivalent)
acceptance_criteria:
1. CLIStagingReader file header or class docstring explains why it exists separately from StagingServiceCore
2. Comment in listReminders() explains why validation failures are silent (no logger in CLI context)
3. No functional changes - documentation only

title: ContextMetricsService: add --no-session-persistence to /context spawn
description:
## Why This Exists

packages/sidekick-daemon/src/context-metrics/context-metrics-service.ts (around line 227) spawns 'claude --session-id <uuid> -p /context' WITHOUT the --no-session-persistence flag. The sibling spawn caller AnthropicCliProvider.complete() in packages/shared-providers/src/providers/anthropic-cli.ts DOES pass --no-session-persistence.

The asymmetry means each context-metrics capture leaves a persisted session behind on disk, which is both unnecessary and inconsistent. Noted as a low-priority cleanup during the --bare investigation (claude-code-sidekick-dm0i) and re-surfaced during implementation of claude-code-sidekick-92qg.

## What Needs to Be Done

1. Add '--no-session-persistence' to the args array in ContextMetricsService.captureBaseMetrics() spawn call.
2. Verify no context-metrics tests regress.

## Acceptance Criteria

Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. ContextMetricsService spawn call includes --no-session-persistence, matching AnthropicCliProvider.

title: Unify VALID_HOOK_NAMES, CLI_COMMAND_TO_HOOK, HOOK_ARG_TO_NAME, and HOOK_NAMES into a single source of truth
description: After sidekick-z63.5 lands, there will be four parallel enumerations of hook names across hook.ts and hook-command.ts: HOOK_NAMES (const tuple, @sidekick/types), VALID_HOOK_NAMES (Set, hook.ts), CLI_COMMAND_TO_HOOK (kebab-to-Pascal map, hook.ts), HOOK_ARG_TO_NAME (kebab+Pascal-to-Pascal map, hook-command.ts). Three of these must be kept in sync by hand every time a hook is added — a clear code smell. Refactor opportunity: derive VALID_HOOK_NAMES from HOOK_NAMES, and generate CLI_COMMAND_TO_HOOK / HOOK_ARG_TO_NAME from a PascalCase-to-kebab-case conversion applied to HOOK_NAMES. Not a blocker; pure hygiene. Discovered while planning sidekick-z63.5.

title: Audit LLM prompts against Prompt Contracts pattern
description: Examine our LLM prompt templates (assets/sidekick/prompts/) against the Prompt Contracts framework from ex-OpenAI engineering. Audit each prompt, identify gaps, and propose improvements where the contract pattern would yield more reliable LLM output.

value: c8w: When extracting hover optimization from React state to DOM-based class toggling, reading refs during render (hoveredRef.current) does NOT trigger re-renders -- the 'O(1)' optimization needs DOM manipulation (querySelectorAll + classList) not ref-reading in JSX. Also: data-tool-use-id attributes + CSS [&_.highlight-class] selectors work well for pair highlighting.

n4f: When decomposing a god-file class into extracted modules, use a StatusFileIO-style interface for DI so the extracted module (doctor-engine) doesn't depend on the original class. The facade class can pass itself as the IO impl since it implements all the methods. Also, vitest 4.x doesn't support pipe in test file name filters — just run them separately or use the exact filename.

osf-session-summary-dedup: Discriminated union result types (SnarkyResult/ResumeResult with status/reason fields) are the right pattern for replacing try/catch with explicit status handling across shared extraction boundaries. The caller wraps with its own policy (logging, error mapping) via switch on status. Also: CoalescingGuard<K> replaces hand-rolled Map+boolean patterns for concurrent request coalescing -- generic, testable, reusable. When decomposing god-functions into step functions, export the steps from the same file (not a new module) so they're testable in isolation while the orchestrator stays thin (~40 LOC). Keep existing integration tests as regression safety net alongside new unit tests per step.

pt2-daemon-decomp-session1: Steps 1-4 of daemon.ts decomposition used bottom-up extraction (leaves first). Key patterns: (1) DI via deps interfaces with callbacks for side effects — same as nq6 TranscriptService pattern. (2) Lazy getters in LogMetricsDeps solved constructor ordering (logger needed early, stateService/taskEngine created later). (3) Delegation shims (private methods proxying to extracted managers) preserved existing type-cast tests without rewriting them. (4) Copilot caught startTime timing semantic drift — when extracting a field to another class, check if initialization timing matters (Date.now() at field decl vs construction point). Inject from original site if timing is load-bearing.

zyk-copilot-catches-real-bugs: Copilot review on zyk PRs found 15 inline comments across 6 PRs. All were real issues: TOCTOU races, optional field rendering, SIGTERM hang risk, silent error swallowing, unnecessary barrel traversal, type-safety loss. This validates the /wait-for-pr-comments workflow as essential — not ceremonial.

cne: Extracting pure functions from React components into utility modules (dateGrouping.ts, transcriptLineHelpers.ts) is the key pattern for testing UI code — keeps components thin, makes logic testable without jsdom.

Pino log files use rotation naming: sidekick.1.log, sidekickd.1.log (not cli.log/sidekickd.log). Pino flattens event payload fields to root level of JSON — no nested payload object. When parsing NDJSON logs, destructure out known Pino metadata fields and treat the rest as payload.

xp4-barrel-reexport-pattern: When extracting a namespace/module from a god-file (e.g. LogEvents from structured-logging.ts), always add re-exports in the original file so existing consumers don't need import path changes. Pattern: export { Foo, bar } from './new-file'. Validated in bead 1bl — zero import changes needed across monorepo.

xp4-pass-through-params: When adding try/catch or error handling to existing code, audit whether function parameters are actually used at call sites. Bead s20 had triggeredBy accepted as a parameter but hard-coded at two call sites — a pre-existing bug exposed by touching the same lines. Copilot caught it.

xp4-simplify-finds-real-issues: The /simplify pass is not ceremonial — it found: (1) WHAT-comments that should be WHY-comments, (2) inconsistent mutating vs non-mutating sort, (3) 18 copy-pasted context spreads extractable to a helper (-102 LOC), (4) narration comments. Always run it, always address findings.

6hc: After /clear, Claude Code writes a <command-name>/clear</command-name> user message to the transcript. The transcript service treated this as a real user prompt (turnCount++, UserPrompt emitted), causing double session analysis. Fix: isExcludedBuiltinCommandInvocation() suppresses UserPrompt events for all 41 excluded builtins. Command OUTPUT (<local-command-stdout>) is still emitted — only INVOCATIONS are suppressed.

xp4-god-file-extraction: When decomposing god-files (1bl, q2e), shared types/helpers go in a separate helpers.ts to avoid circular deps. Extracted modules import from helpers.ts, never from the original index.ts. Re-export from index.ts for backward compat.

xp4-parallel-merge-conflicts: When merging 7+ PRs that touch overlapping files (e.g. eslint.config.js, structured-logging.ts), merge them sequentially not in parallel. Later PRs may need rebasing. PR #131 conflicted after #128 removed an eslint ignore pattern.

zyk-agents-stop-after-simplify: Agents dispatched with /simplify in their workflow consistently stop after the simplify pass without committing/pushing/creating PRs. Worktree agents are more reliable (7/9 created PRs) vs main-tree-bleed agents (0/2). For future dispatches: (1) always use worktree isolation, (2) add BOLD warnings that simplify is NOT the last step, (3) explicitly state DO NOT STOP AFTER SIMPLIFY.

mh2: vi.hoisted() is required for mock variables referenced inside vi.mock() factory functions in vitest 4.x. Static methods on mocked classes must be explicitly preserved by copying them from the actual module onto the mock constructor (cast to any).

xp4-coverage-threshold-on-extraction: When extracting code from a tested file into a new file, the new file inherits low/zero test coverage and may trip coverage thresholds. This is a pre-existing gap, not a regression. Bead q2e: scripted.ts extracted at 56% line coverage because original tests targeted setup/index.ts.

1dq: When replacing inline settings file reads (readFile + JSON.parse) with a shared utility, watch for callers that need to distinguish 'file missing' from 'file has bad JSON' -- a utility that swallows all errors silently may drop important error logging. In cleanSettingsFile (uninstall.ts), using readFileOrNull + inline JSON.parse preserved the original warn-on-corrupt-JSON behavior that readSettingsFile would have swallowed.

b5m: DEFAULT_LATENCY_STATS uses Infinity for min, but Zod z.number() rejects Infinity. This is safe because DEFAULT_LATENCY_STATS is only used in-memory and never persisted via Zod-validated state writes. If it were ever persisted, the schema would need z.number().or(z.literal(Infinity)) or a transform.

xp4-extraction-field-dropping: When extracting functions from god-files, optional fields in object params can get silently dropped if the agent narrows the type during extraction. PR #131 lost reminderText from reminderStaged and renderedText/hookInput from statuslineRendered. Always diff the original function signature against the extracted version to verify all fields preserved.

xp4-worktree-branch-conflict: When dispatching fix-up agents to existing PR branches, the branch may already be checked out in a prior worktree. The new agent gets a fresh worktree but cannot git checkout the branch. Either clean up old worktrees first, or have the fix-up agent work in the existing worktree via SendMessage.

nq6-bulkstate-followup: The TranscriptService decomposition (PR #151) left one code smell: the bulkState parameter bag in transcript-file-watcher.ts processTranscriptFile() uses getter/setter proxies with a no-this-alias disable comment. The bulk processing lifecycle (isBulkProcessing, hasFiredBulkComplete, hasBacklogAtPrepareTime, bulkStartTime) should either be extracted into a BulkProcessingState class or moved back into the coordinator since it's lifecycle-management state, not file-watching state. Identified by code-reviewer agent.

ef0: When extracting a closure from a method into a private method, pass captured variables as explicit parameters. The colorizeToken closure captured branchColor/viewModel/personaColor — these became method params. Also: export type re-exports don't bring the type into local scope in TypeScript; you still need a separate import for local usage.

x43: When extracting helpers from deeply nested handlers, the linter's post-edit hook rejects unused imports immediately — you MUST update both the import AND the usage in one atomic edit, or the linter will revert the file. Also, when working in a worktree, Read/Edit tools use the file_path as given — ensure paths point to the worktree, not the main tree. Copying files after the fact works but is wasteful.

xvc: When splitting a barrel into domain files, cross-domain unions (SidekickEvent = HookEvent | TranscriptEvent) must stay in the barrel to avoid circular deps between the domain files. Domain files should only import downward (transcript-events imports from hook-events, not vice versa). The barrel re-exports everything from domain files plus defines the cross-domain glue.

30q-stale-closure-in-coalescing-guard: When wrapping a function with CoalescingGuard, the guard replays the same fn closure on coalesced reruns. If the closure captures mutable state (like countdown/bookmark), the rerun uses stale values. Fix: reload mutable state inside the guarded function, not outside. The old hand-rolled guard explicitly reloaded state before reruns; the generic guard can't know to do this. Copilot caught this on PR #149.

xp4-agent-memory-compliance: Of 7 agents instructed to run bd remember on close, only 1 did. Memory protocol in bead notes is necessary but not sufficient — agents skip optional-feeling steps under time pressure. For zyk epic: make bd remember a hard prerequisite before bd close, or have the orchestrator capture memories post-completion.
